reviews.text: New and Improve amazing gym! Been coming here for a long time and got re done and looks amazing now after all the changes. Courtney is amazing and she is always helping people and making sure the gym is great! Come check it out!

I have been coming to this 24 hour fitness consistently for about 5 years. It has been a great experience of course that’s why I keep coming back. What I like most about this 24hr fitness on Richmond is the great views of the city with the floor to ceiling windows. It has been annoying that the stair master machine has been out for several months. I guess they are working on upgrades for the gym so they are holding off on fixing them. There is a jacuzzi, pool, sauna and steam room at this location. There is usually a good vibe. I don’t come in after 4pm because that is when there is way too many people for me. I have train with some of the trainers here but they don’t seem to stay long so that’s unfortunate. It also seems to be new management every year so I’m not sure what that’s about. All in all I would recommend people come in and check it out.

I’ve had a great experience at this gym, and I especially want to recognize Nanetta Smith and Alondra. Both are consistently professional, welcoming, and helpful whenever I’m there. They create a positive environment and clearly care about the members and how the gym is run. Whether it’s answering questions or just being approachable and friendly, they really make a difference. Employees like them are a big reason I enjoy coming here. Highly appreciate their hard work and great customer service.

Courtney was the first person to speak about the gym new stuff and showing the area showing massages area opening November 1st before there almost done for the reopen section it was great to see Richmond location as been open for business thank you all staff thank you Courtney. 😊

Easily the worst gym I've ever worked out in.  Simply put, staff do not care about cleanliness or organization.  Weights of all sizes everywhere.  I spend more time looking for matching dumbells than I did working out.

name: LA Fitness
address: 7620 Katy Fwy Ste 200, Houston, TX 77024, USA

reviews.text:
The equipment here is so bad.

I cant believe how bad this gym is.

Its so old, half the equipment is rusted or broken in some minor way. A ton of the equipment is super outdated.

Treadmills and bikes upstairs are all rusted with paint chipping.

The ac in this gym is stuffy and old.

You would think this gym being as popular as it is would have up to date equipment.

Its just a really gross and old gym.

Yansa at the Front desk at LA Fitness is absolutely amazing. From the moment you walk in, she’s welcoming, knowledgeable, and genuinely eager to help. She goes above and beyond to make sure members feel comfortable, supported, and informed, never rushed, never dismissive.

You can tell she takes real pride in her work. Whether it’s answering questions, helping with memberships, or just creating a positive atmosphere, Yansa does it all with professionalism and a great attitude. LA Fitness is lucky to have someone as hardworking and dedicated as her. Highly recommend asking for Yansa if you need help!
One of the oldest LA fitness gym’s and the customer service was awful. Please avoid this gym if you can. There’s a great one down the street on Yale.

Estella is absolutely amazing. I have been to a lot of LA Fitness stores and not once have I seen someone with her customer service/positivity for the early morning shift. It is always nice to walk in and see her smiling face!

I have worked out at numerous LA Fitness gyms in multiple states since 1994 and this is one of the worst. Appeared understaffed. Gym and men's locker room filthy. The entire gym and equipment is very dated. A female staff member walked into the men's locker room unannounced (unprofessional).

name: Berry Center of Northwest Houston
address: 8877 Barker Cypress Rd, Cypress, TX 77433, USA

reviews.text: I was here for state wrestling. Pretty cool place. Clean bathrooms foood was just okay. If you’re on the bigger side like myself the chairs will be uncomfortable. Overall nice place plenty of parking.
Wonderful fun filled family experience.  🎄
Went for Life Surge we had so much fun. Beautiful facility!!

Love that they have good food. Chick-fil-A, Cane's, Brisket, burger, drinks could cost less but that's the only thing. I shouldn't be paying beer prices for soft drink, water, and Gatorade. Then again probably don't need to drink that much soda.

It is a huge center with multiple ways to go up and down.Thankfully there were sufficient elevators.
Large ladies' restrooms helped the flow greatly. Well lit and easy to go up and down the bleachers with lots of places to hold on.

9:45 AM. I showed up because Google says this gym should be open… and it’s closed.

Honestly, it’s frustrating because it’s not just the service or the condition of the place—it’s that they don’t even stick to their own hours. You pay for a membership, plan your morning, drive all the way here, and they waste your time like it doesn’t matter.

And before anyone tries to blame the weather or a “storm warning”: there’s no snow here. Yeah, it’s a little chilly, but nothing extreme. I was recently in New York with heavy snow and gyms were still open. Even Costco is open today, but this gym isn’t.

This is just disrespectful. You’re paying for a service and still losing days because of poor management. If they can’t even follow their own schedule, what are we paying for?

I have been a loyal La Fitness member for years, and I want to give you guys an honest review of this location.

Before you get a membership.
1. This location’s management is awful. If you scroll down the review section, the owner only responds to “good reviews” because they don’t actually address the real problems this location has.
2. It takes on average 5-6 (if not longer) months to get simple machines fixed. For example, the basketball gym only has one rim since Q2 of this year, and it's now late December.
3. This gym can get disgustingly dirty at times. You can tell by the leftover sweat marks on the cardio machines and the lack of employees who clean the location. They expect YOU to wipe down the machine, which is fine, but other gyms usually cycle areas that employees clean daily.
4. There is no structure to the workflow of their employees (which stems from bad ownership and management)
-Front desk employees get no formal training. 90% of the time, they are just on their phones, and at times, there could be 2-3 of them up there, just on their phones, instead of being productive and helping in other areas around the gym.
- I honestly don't know what the cleaning crew does. There are times when I have to circle the entire gym to get sanitizer wipes, and they're all empty. There are times when they just put a spray bottle and paper towels out (because management didn't order wipes). I go to this location frequently at different times of day, and I have yet to see them clean any sections of the gym.

Before you get a new “promotional” membership for 2026, please consider my review.
Here on vacation. Fair La fitness but could definitely go for some renovations. I’ll be back in here tomorrow. Gets the job done just fine

LA Fitness is a big chain, but at this location it’s obvious how far things have gone downhill. What’s worse is seeing how, because of the irresponsibility of the people they put in charge, instead of improving, everything just keeps getting worse. I came all the way here and IT WAS CLOSED with no prior notice, and on top of that Google still shows the place as “open.” That’s completely disrespectful—of your time, your money, and the trust you put in a place like this. And to make it even worse, basic courtesy and professionalism seem nonexistent here. This gym needs a lot of fixing—and not just the facility, but the people running it.

I’ve been going to LA Fitness for a while now, and overall it’s a great spot to stay active. The equipment is well-maintained, and there’s a wide variety of machines and free weights. I especially appreciate the group classes—they keep me motivated and add fun to my routine. If you’re looking for a full-service gym with flexible hours and a good vibe, LA Fitness is a solid choice. Thanks Rick
Love the Swim Classes offered! Instructors were very good at getting kids to pay attention to learn a new skill. They have progress reports and different levels to find the right spot for each kid.

While our initial experience with this YMCA was great, they have unfortunately changed management too many times. The inconsistency and lack of response have been unacceptable. Their child night watch was something to look forward to, but recently they took our money for it, had parents show up to drop their kids off, only to be told it was cancelled with no notice. They recently increased their charge to $40/child for three hours of child watch. It took two months for a refund for the mistake. I recently attempted to cancel my membership. Their calls go unanswered. This is a very unresponsive location, and I will be going to another location going forward.

This is a poorly organized location with a lack of attention to its members. Shame, as their swimming coaches are fantastic and the facilities are clean. Their customer service and response are under par.

This community does not need made-up, self-imposed, or subjective policies

On Monday, August 11, my husband and I were at the gym for our second guest pass session, and we had an online training session with our trainer. A staff member named Ashley approached us and told us that we could not use our mobile phones in the gym area. We explained that we were using our phones for online training, but Ashley repeatedly said it wasn’t allowed and claimed it was the gym’s policy. When we asked her to show us the policy, she did not provide anything because no such policy exists, not even on the gym’s website. Ashley’s behavior was extremely unprofessional and disrespectful, and she even stopped our session in the middle of the class.

My husband emailed three gym managers, Monica, Andrew, and Lesly, whose email addresses are publicly listed on the gym’s website. None of them responded until my husband informed them that we would be taking action through our legal representative. Only then did they reply to our emails.
Andrew asked my husband to come to the gym on Saturday, August 17, to review the supposed policy. My husband went, and guess what? The manager, Mr. Andrew, did not show up at all!

We are currently in the process of filing a legal complaint against this gym. If you value respect and fair treatment, you should think twice before trusting this gym with your membership.

The YMCA of Houston is wonderful! So many activities and classes to choose from, e.g., water aerobics, stretch, yoga, pilates, barre, cardio and weight equipment, swimming as well as outside activities. It's a fun, clean place to get in shape and meet new people.

The Group Classes are Awesome! Staff is Friendly. Love this Facility! My favorite classes are all of the Yoga, mat pilates & Les Mills Body classes especially BodyPump. Stretch and Restore is great too!

name: Life Time
address: 7717 Willow Chase Blvd, Houston, TX 77070, USA

reviews.text: The woman who works the 4am morning shift at this location during the week is always so kind and welcoming. She has such a positive energy and really sets the tone for a great workout and an even better day. Being greeted with a smile every morning truly makes a difference. I really appreciate her and the consistency she brings.
I really love all the classes I come to and the community of friends I have made. Coach Nico, Jeremy & Carolyn have great energy and are very informative when helping you out achieve your goals.

The daycare staff and trainers at this facility are excellent, professional, friendly, and highly skilled. Unfortunately, the overall experience is undermined by poor management. There are frequent issues with basic operations, such as the lack of clean towels available and insufficient downtime scheduled for proper maintenance. The manager, Dallas, has been consistently unprofessional and dismissive in interactions. Additionally, key amenities like the pool, hot tub, steam room, and sauna are often unavailable without clear communication. Guests are frequently told to “wait 30 minutes” with no guarantee of resolution. These recurring issues significantly detract from what could otherwise be a great facility.

I was recently sideswiped in the parking lot by one of their contracted security guards. Unfortunately, the driver didn't accept responsibility for the fault and didn't have insurance. I got told by the staff that one of the managers was going to reach out to provide me with camera footage but they never did. Although, the security guard works for a third-party vendor LT should really look into who they hire and have responsible personnel to represent their brand.

Other than that I've been a frequent member for the past five years and have seen the management team try to improve the quality of the gym. It sucks that something preventable could change my mind on the organization.

The community nurtured by the team members and members drives the experience at Life Time at Champions. The people make this place great. The instructors love to teach members in high energy classes, and personal trainers are there to help you reach your goals or just educate on how to use the equipment safely.

name: BreakAway Speed Sports Specific Training, LP
address: 8715 W Loop S suite e, Houston, TX 77096, USA

reviews.text: THIS IS LEGIT TRAINING ! came in town for a few days and needed some offseason training (Pro CFL player) . Jeremy and Coach Randy know what they’re doing and the gym had a great spirit to it. There program is fool proof and works for all athletes , of all gender and all age ranges.
My daughter loves Breakaway Speed! She loves doing private softball lessons with Coach Randy and then sticking around for the agility classes. Everyone is so friendly and helpful! Highly recommend!
My son is doing their speed and agility classes.  He loves them and we are noticing a difference in the way he's moving.
Great cages at an affordable price. Machines always work and the customer service is the best in houston

I highly recommend Melvin C. for personal training. He's fantastic, and he knows what he's doing! I played sports for 24 years, but then had several kids and stopped. I trained with Melvin after 10 years of sitting (driving and teaching at a desk), living a sedentary lifestyle, and struggling with several joint limitations, back pain, and prior sports injuries. I honestly wasn't sure if I could get back in shape, but I knew IF it was possible, I'd need a trainer to design a very specific plan for me. I had asked for that with prior trainers at different gyms and was never able to get it.  No one wanted to take the time to develop a plan for me to follow inside and outside the gym. But Melvin took the challenge and exceeded my expectations! He took time to listen to my situation, test my injuries and abilities without hurting them, find weaknesses, develop a plan to target those weaknesses in a secondary fashion (until they were strong enough to be targeted directly), and add in total body fitness alongside it. He has gone above and beyond any personal trainer I've ever used before.

Honestly, I've never seen half the stuff he has shown me, and I've done a lot over the years! He has worked with an Olympic trainer the last three years to develop his own skills, has a martial arts background that adds to his understanding of motion and body movement, has a sports background so that his sports conditioning program is excellent (my teens used him as well to improve their game), and he has taught me some new, highly effective warm ups and post-exercise stretches.

He's great with teaching proper form so that the right muscles are being targeted in each activity, and lets you know if you're accidentally slipping out of form and working some other area by accident. And the results are undeniable.  After only 4-5 weeks of working with him, I was shocked at how my mobility, range of motion, strength, and overall ability to do exercise had improved.  I highly recommend Melvin to anyone who needs specialized training of any sort.  He's excellent!!

the dirtiest gym I've ever been to, I don't know what happened to them? I was there before the pandemic and it was very clean, organized, and well maintained, but now it's horrible! I was in another one from other companies, but they closed the one closest to my house, and I came back 24 hrs, It was a mistake, because they immediately closed that one too, (westheimer location)  but the worst thing is that they don't have a cleaning staff. It's unfair that the only person at the front desk has to do everything! It's impossible! The equipment is full of mold and many have sweat marks from a long time, it smells really bad, and the locker rooms and showers are always flooded, the trash cans are always full, the paper dispensers are always empty, and the hot room is also too dirty, they never clean it, and it smells disgusting. Also, I signed up because they offered classes and suddenly there are none! I waited a few months to post this bad review because I thought it was temporary, but it wasn't! And I really think I'll change because we deserve a clean place that smells good when we exercise. I don't usually do this, but I hope they take it into account.

I took my mom her to check out the membership the front desk employees were amazing helpful friendly and fun. They were so nice to my senior mom. They made her day they made her laugh and feel better about herself again.  Brian Vern Shashua are an ASSET to 24 Hour Fitness.
I love when my mom joined and Brian told her he is happy she is here . No words can express my appreciation today.
Thank you 24 Hour Fitness for great customer service.

I only like the workout machines and air conditioning at this 24hr Fitness Dairy Ashford location. The men's lockers were falling apart. The bathroom area was deteriorated and extremely stinky. The wet area was half functioning every time I visit. The operating hours were extremely weird. Friday to Sunday are not 24 hours.
Love this gym, all the staff are friendly. Rome has always been super welcoming and helpful with anything I need. The gym is kept well and is big enough where it never feels overly packed.

Bern is an amazing employee at 24 Hour Fitness. He’s always friendly, professional, and genuinely helpful every time I’m at the gym. You can tell he actually cares about members and takes pride in what

name: Life Time
address: 815 Town and Country Blvd, Houston, TX 77024, USA

reviews.text:
I absolutely love everything about Lifetime CityCentre! Every class is such a joy, and the energy from the instructors is truly inspiring. Jared, our Studio Leader, doesn’t just lead the team — he genuinely cares about each member and instructor, making everyone feel supported and motivated. Every instructor I’ve met is amazing (too many great names to mention😁🤣♥️), bringing passion, positivity, and fun to every session.

The Concierge Team is always so friendly and helpful, and Membership Concierge Leader, Fahad Alsammarraie, is simply outstanding — always going above and beyond, making sure every member feels welcomed, valued, and taken care of. Their dedication truly makes a difference in everyone’s experience💯

This gym isn’t just a place to work out — it’s a community full of warmth, encouragement, and inspiration. I honestly consider Lifetime CityCentre my second home. ✨🏠🤗

First impressions are everything.

I’ve been with Life Time for a number of years.

The front desk team; from Freddy & Kim, to Leo, Gabi, Grace, Steven, Roxanna,
Michael and Cindy…they have become friends and more like extended family.

A big shout to Mike and his Cafe team!

Great teams are derived from great people.

Great people provide great service.

Thank you for being you!

Marc and Dorjon at Life Time Fitness were absolutely amazing. Both were incredibly friendly, professional, and helpful throughout my visit. They went out of their way to make sure I felt comfortable, answered all my questions, and provided excellent service with a great attitude. You can tell they truly care about members and take pride in what they do. Their positivity and work ethic really stood out and made my experience at Life Time even better. Highly recommend recognizing both of them for outstanding service.

An amazing place where you can improve your health & energy, enhance your mobility, flexibility, strength  and make a positive impact on you life, I strongly recommend scheduling an appointment with coach Tarek so you can discover your true potential or simply feel much better at every working session especially if you are recovering from injuries or such you will save thousands in doctors or medicines.
Such a wonderful facility! The staff is so friendly and helpful. You won’t find a better place for PB. Yoga is great-  took a Boga Board Yoga class with Mary that was amazing, fun and challenging!

Timberline is a fantastic gym, and I couldn’t recommend it more.  This family-owned gem is a standout.  My wife and I have been members for about 4 years, and it has been a refreshing change from the big chain fitness centers.

The atmosphere is welcoming and friendly. The staff truly cares about your fitness, and it shows in the attention to detail and the positive energy that fills the space. Whether you're a beginner or a seasoned athlete, you feel right at home.

The gym itself is impeccably clean, and everything is well-maintained. From the cardio equipment to the weight area, everything works perfectly, and you can tell that the owners take pride in ensuring the space is always in top-notch condition. It’s a space built with care and designed for success.

One of the best things about Timberline Fitness is its community feel. It’s a place where you’re not just another number. Everyone—from the staff to the members—is friendly, supportive, and welcoming.

It is the best gym in town.

As a track athlete, Timberline Fitness has drastically improved my strength and running performance. I began training with Garrett in June of 2024 in order to prevent the injuries that I would commonly experience with running. I began seeing improvement in both my body and speed very quickly. Garrett is an extremely easygoing and laidback trainer, but still pushes me when I need motivation. Working out at a gym like Timberline not only gives you a great environment, but also many great resources. My personal favorite, after hard track or lifting workouts, is the cold plunge. This is a new, unique resource that’s not available at many large gyms. It is my favorite because of how much it helps with my recovery.
My strength has improved greatly after working out consistently at Timberline and I highly recommend it to anyone looking to begin, or continue their fitness journey .

I have been training at Timberline for a number of years now and the experience remains second to none. The trainers (Dean, Garrett and others) are friendly, professional, and have the experience to push you just enough for you to continue to improve, build strength and make gains session after session.

There are no egos at this gym. The culture, the trainers, the other members - everyone is there to cheer each other on and celebrate the successes!

I’ve been working out at Timberline Fitness for years, and I can’t say enough good things about this gym and the awesome family behind it! From the moment I walked in, I loved its cozy, boutique vibe—not an overwhelming sea of treadmills, but still packed with all the equipment I could ever need. I started training with Garrett twice a week and never stopped! The genuine care and family atmosphere keep me motivated, making this the most consistent I’ve ever been with my fitness routine.

I’ve been working out at Timberline for about a year and I couldn’t be happier with my experience. Garrett and Dean are always finding new additions and equipment to improve/expand the facilities. But even better than the workouts is the Timberline community. Easily the best gym in Houston!

name: Weekley Family YMCA
address: 7101 Stella Link Rd, Houston, TX 77025, USA

reviews.text: I like The layout of our facility, the whirlpool, steam and dry sauna and pool area. I also like having the rooms for individual workouts. Our steam room is one of the feet’s that have it. Also, having the daycare, restaurant and security make it like our own little country club with a variety of amenities. There is a new app out that I will download but I did like the previous app.

We joined after being told multiple times that a year membership included one free month as part of the promotion. Because we were mid-month, staff advised us to pay the first month and get the next month free, and we were told we qualified before and during sign-up.

Five months later, we’ve been charged every month. I followed up monthly, and when we finally emailed the director, Tiffany, the email was ignored and we were then told we never qualified because the promo was only for Impact-to-regular members—something never disclosed.

Had this been communicated honestly, we wouldn’t have committed to a full year. Poor communication and lack of accountability were very disappointing.

Great place to workout. They have 2-3 people walking around always cleaning the used machines.

Reading the reviews I have to say that they wipe down the mats after every use. You must wear a mask unless you are doing cardio. If you want to wear you can.  There are bottles of cleaner and paper towels thru out the gym.

Gym has full equipment. Every bike and treadmill has cable and screens with aps.

No join fee and 55 a month is a steal when you compare it to other places.  Saturday is half day so it gets busy and most machines are full.

I’ll start off by saying nice things about it. The equipment is top notch & the facility is fairly clean on the wellness floor and through most of the place. Most staff are very kind. Pool area is solid and the steam room works wonderfully.

My issue stems with the enforcement of rules and non-rules. The trainers are great and very accommodating of other patrons’s space. However, kids can run amuck & people can throw dumbbells around. However when I deadlift on the Olympic platform well within the rules, leave it to two wellness floor employees to single me out & interrupt me mid-set every single time.

Please for the love of God, upgrade the lighting in this facility. It feels like a stuffy prison more than a gym. All the other YMCAs I’ve been to are well lit. Especially the Gymnasium. Good for mole people, bad for sports.

1 star. I specifically have a LA Fitness membership so I can play basketball, yet every single time I come here, the basketball court is closed. What am I paying for if the main reason I joined is never available? There's no consistency, no clear schedule, and no communication. If you're going to advertise amenities, they should actually be open. Very disappointing....IF I COULD GIVE 0 STARS I WOULD!!!! DO BETTER AND STOP ALL THIS FALSE ADVERTISING!!!

Cancelled my membership today after months of being lied to from my home location about the Sauna being fixed since September of 2024 (currently June 2025)

The closest location with a working Sauna if this one but the rest of the gym is falling apart and is poorly maintained (various ceiling panels missing due to leaks, buckets and totes all over your collect rain leaks). In addition to the to unsafe conditions, weights and equipment is always out of place and staff makes no effort to organize or manage (granted everyone should be putting back the weights they use).

Membership services was no help and actually encouraged me to cancel my membership.

Very dissatisfied, they have been reported to the BBB.

It's disappointing because I feel scammed by this gym. I paid for the membership online, went, and didn't like the gym because the machines were old and broken. I canceled the membership immediately, and on top of that, they charged me again for the amount I had already paid. This means I paid for the annual membership twice.

If I could give this 0 stars I would . LA fitness as a brand is one of the most cheapest companies I have ever been . You guys charge full price . Yet all of your locations have more than a handful of equipment out of service .

Honestly has potential, but the people working the desk and front have no clue or care about anything. God forbid you miss a payment, they will, let you in; then come in there, out on the floor and will dress you down right there, and force you to leave or pay. Like why couldn't this be handled at the desk before I got out here with my group In front of everyone. It seems very unprofessional. This could have been handled before I got in there, and got started, and took my pre workout ; and then you dress me down in front of everyone and force me to leave. Then they can't make up their minds about if they take cash. I leave to go get money and come back to pay, and then I am still denied. All the while, for almost 6 months they had leaking ceilings where half equipment was unusable and more than half the ceiling tiles were missing. Looking like a ten dollar club. Water would pour in when it rained and don't get me started on the bathrooms and locker rooms with no ceilings and buckets everywhere for months. They charge you like a high end club, and then treat you, and the maintenance like it's a it's a burden. If you're looking for a new gym , I would recommend any location but this one.  Very unprofessional and half the time there is no one at the front desk when you walk in. Most of the people that work there spend their time's chit chatting not paying attention to anything anyway. What is the support of the security when half the people walk in with no check. God forbid you get there at 5 AM. And at least once or twice a week the opening crew is late. I cannot wait to cancel my membership. The cleaning people are so great. The shining stars there.

Ive observed Nick and Darian provide top tier customer service on a few occasions. On my visits at the katy location I am typically welcomed with a bright smile and friendly greeting. Anytime I’ve needed assistance with my membership , these guys don’t hesitate to lend a helping hand. I love LA FITNESS & have marked this location as my all time favorite!!

i am very sad to make this review , as i have been a member for man years but after several times of bringing this issue up to management and front desk and seeing nothing is done about it , it leaves me no choice. This is extremely disgusting and horrible for the health , the pool has mold growing around the pool i can only imagine what is in the pool or if they even clean it. i have developed an illness due to this. the pool area even smells like mold!!! PLEASE FIX THIS ISSUE ASAP!!!! YOUR MEMBERS HEALTH IS IMPORTANT

This branch is pretty good for what it is, it has an adequate amount of weight and equipment. Nicholas in the front makes the gym feel like a welcoming atmosphere. His pleasant greetings and warm attitude when we walk in sets the tone for a good workout day

New management was desperately needed at this location and the new manager is excellent. If you’re looking for a new gym or you are a member and haven’t been in to this location in a while give them a chance. They could still use an upgrade on the dumbbell rack, but from what I’ve been told it’s in the works. Gym management makes a huge difference, and again, new management is excellent.

The front desk service was hesitant to let me in. I had a 3 day pass, and he said I should come in at 9 am. Anyway, he took my ID and let me in. The gym was practically empty because I went really early in the morning. The gym looked a little dirty, and some of the benches had sweat stains. THIS PLACE DO NOT BELIEVE IN RE-RACK YOUR WEIGHTS. I have never seen a gym so bad at this. Dumbells all over the place. 120lb Dumbells on the ground from days before. Plates were everywhere but where they were supposed to be.  I took some pictures. Re-rack your weights!! I got my work out in.

name: Motherhood Center -Pregnancy Wellness/Education Center and Doula/Nanny Placement Agency
address: 3701 W Alabama St Suite 230, Houston, TX 77027, USA

reviews.text: Truly one of the best massages I have ever had! Rosie was so kind, explained how prenatal massages differ from regular massages prior to starting the massage, and overall was just a lovely person. I had a wonderful experience from the moment I walked in the door. I will definitely be going back.

I have had the best experience at The Motherhood Center. The location is easy to find, parking is great, and the staff is amazing and always so helpful. I’ve been seeing Tonja for prenatal massages since my second trimester, and she is incredible. I genuinely look forward to every appointment and always leave feeling relaxed and refreshed. This is such a wonderful treat and absolutely worth the visit.

I did 2 classes to help prepare for our first baby. I found them extremely helpful and provided information that I’m not sure I would have otherwise ever known. My husband participated in both classes and he was actually engaged in both. They even provided beer towards the end of class which was a highlight for him 😂 Our instructor, Angie, taught both classes and she was incredible. She is a nurse and a doula (for over 15 years) so she is highly qualified to lead the classes. We left feeling more prepared and confident. I highly recommend!

I just got the most fantastic prenatal massage from Iris. I’ve been experiencing  sciatic nerve issues through pregnancy and Iris’s work released some major tension significantly reducing my pain! Cannot recommend her enough to pregnant mamas. I’m looking forward to learning more about the other resources at the center!

They have been an amazing support in my pregnancy. I love the Pilates & fitness class the coach is the best and you feel great after the workout. Yoga classes are also very nice, empowering and relaxing.
All you need to enjoy and feel strong in this beautiful season 🤰🏼

I’ve been going to this LA Fitness in Pearland for almost a year and love it. I can walk there, the parking lot is well lit and feels safe, and the gym is always clean with well-maintained equipment. The staff is friendly and welcoming, especially the manager, Chris, who is polite, approachable, and very knowledgeable. My daughter also loves the childcare area. Overall, a great gym and a great experience!

LA Fitness in pearland is hands down one of the best gyms I’ve been to, and a big reason for that is the management. Chris is absolutely the best—professional, approachable, and truly invested in creating a positive experience for members. You can tell he cares about the facility, the staff, and the people who walk through the doors. The gym is well run, welcoming, and consistently maintained. Highly recommend this location if you’re looking for a great atmosphere.

This is one of our go to gyms but it could truly use a remodel. With the opening of crunch they did get some new equipment but it truly could use a remodel. The patrons are all nice and supportive. Staff is always nice and welcoming too.

LA Fitness is the absolute best!!!  My mom has been going for years and I came up with her on a buddy pass.  They’ve got the new booty builder machines in and oooooh do ya feel the burn!!!  The people who work there are amazing and the personal trainers are fantastic!!!!  I’ve seen results in just one week!  Plus, I just moved back to Pearland from Colorado and love seeing all my old Pearland friends I’ve known since elementary school come in to work out.  It’s like a
Pearland High School reunion up in here!  Ask for Chris!  He and/or She is The Best!!!!  Love the classes and the hot tub jets are super powerful, especially on your feet.  Chow, see ya up there!

HORRIBLE EXPERIENCE – Final Draw (Cancelled Membership)

I’ve been a member here for many years, but things have gone downhill badly. Most of the amenities have been removed or are constantly broken. The bathing suit dryer was taken out a long time ago, and the jacuzzi has been unreliable for over a year — especially the main "massaging" jet, which rarely works. When it’s fixed, something else breaks within days.

My final experience sealed it. I paid $20 for a guest pass to use the jacuzzi (which is pretty much all I use there, since I work out elsewhere). Within seconds of paying, I saw the sign saying the jacuzzi was broken again. I immediately asked for a refund, but Chris Carlile (Sales Manager) refused, even though he had just told me earlier he would refund it if there was an issue. When I called him out for poor customer service, he decided to “teach me a lesson” by not honoring his word.

I even tried calling back later to resolve it politely before leaving this review, and disputing my credit card and emailing corporate -but no luck as he is avoiding my calls.

Crunch Fitness opens in a few weeks — I strongly recommend checking them out instead(its literally across the street)(. At least there, customer service and working amenities might actually matter.

Thank you, Chris, for teaching me how to dispute a charge and write reviews — I never write them, but this time I had to. I hope u can do better to the next person.

I will call BBB on wednesday when i have more time, and I recommend you do as well if you have any grievance with this particular LA fitness.

This is the weight rack on a Saturday morning. ALL the weights are gone.

Apparently they were being used for a class. If this was LA fitness, totally understand. This is Lifetime where you pay hundreds of dollars in membership. If weights are needed for a class, the gym should have extra weights rather than using all the free weights in the general area.

“Sugarland Lifetime: We charge Lifetime prices and deliver Planet Fitness services.”

*Side note, staff are all really nice people. Super helpful and always friendly :)

I was really sad to hear that Ashley is no longer working at this location. She was one of the most hardworking and caring instructors here. She truly cared about the members and the staff, always made everyone feel welcome, and was very motivating. You could really feel her passion, and she made a big difference in helping people stay healthy and consistent.
It’s hard to understand why someone who gave so many years to this club and clearly cared so much would be let go. Ashley meant a lot to many of us, and it’s disappointing that member feedback didn’t seem to be taken into account.
I hope management reflects on this and considers how important long-term, dedicated employees like Ashley and others are to the members and the overall community.
On a side note, this location already feels different from other Lifetime Fitness locations, especially when it comes to maintenance and cleanliness.

We liked Sugar Land LifeTime so far. But of recently, the new manager has been kicking out good instructors and cancelling good classes. we liked the strength training class by Ashley Murphy who was let go, even though she was a very good instructor. There was a good thursday yoga class by Hoda, which was also yanked off. Recently the prices were hiked too. But still the gym has been slashing good instructors and classes. It is all happened after the new Studio Manager hired.

To add further, Yoga class by Hoda has been reduced to 45 mins now. I think thats not enough for yoga atleast given that first 15 mins and last 15 mins are mostly warm up and cool down, leaving only 15 mins of core yoga.
Unfortunately, these reviews wont be looked by anyone higher up the corporate.

I have been a member of this club for more than 15 years. They recently  wrongfully fired the best instructor they have Ashley Murphy.

Ashley has been an integral part of this club’s identity and a major contributor to member satisfaction, retention, and long-term engagement. Her leadership, professionalism, and unwavering dedication have directly shaped the success of the group fitness program and have fostered a loyal community that many members consider irreplaceable.

Her removal — without communication, transparency, or reasonable justification — has negatively impacted numerous members who have relied on her instruction, motivation, consistency, and expertise.
Please bring her back

I’ve been a member for over 10 years. One of the the highlights for me were the extraordinary group fitness classes led by Thais and Ashley. Ashley Murphy has been an award winning instructor multiple times over and always a kind, warm, positive human being. But, moreover, a fabulous coach and high quality fitness instructor who would always encourage and push us in all the best ways possible.

Thais led my favorite spin classes! It was a highlight of my week to be in a room with her generous spirit, high energy music, and challenging routines. She was fired and has been and continues to be a great lose for Lifetime Sugar Land.

Now the latest casualty is Ashley Murphy. Who very recently was fired for no reason. I am one of many people who can see this club in decline. And yet rates for membership keep going up!

Please join me in telling the local management and corporate that they need to truly reconsider hiring back two of their best.

Absolute BEST gym in Houston.  I’ve been a member for a few years and I have never considered other options.  Even when busy it is tolerable and people are very nice.  It is super clean, great equipment, and just small enough to feel comfortable.  It is a simple gym, but it has everything you could need.  If you are looking for a friendly place with equipment that is on par with any big gym this place is for you!  For a smaller gym they have an insane amount of power/squat racks.

Better than any gym I’ve been to. Boxing gym, hole-in-walls, big names like 24hr, PF, etc. maybe it’s the people or the location. The coaches maybe? I don’t have to wait to use a machine. It’s a vibe here. I’m closer to my fitness goals than I have ever been and I’m not sure if I’d have been able to do it anywhere else. Their app is also amazing!!

I visit this Anytime Fitness location twice a year when I’m in town visiting family, and overall, it’s one of the best Anytime Fitness gyms I’ve been to. The facility is spotless, well-maintained, and has a great selection of equipment compared to other locations. I also appreciate the extra touches, like offering massage guns for members to use.

The only downside is the barbell area. It’s pretty cramped, and there’s no deadlift platform, which makes it very easy to create noise during heavy lifts. They use Kevlar plates instead of rubber bumper plates, which aren’t ideal for deadlifting without a platform — a swap to rubber plates would be a vast improvement here.

While deadlifting during my visit, the owner asked me to keep the noise down. I asked if there were mats or any other way to better pad the floor, but no solution was offered. Even while deadlifting with controlled form, it was nearly impossible to stay quiet unless I slowed the negative phase of the lift to around three seconds, which isn’t practical when lifting above 80%of your max.

Later, I noticed another squat rack in a different area of the gym, where the floor seemed a bit more durable. In the future, I would choose that spot for deadlifting instead.

Despite the challenges in the barbell area, this gym is excellent overall, and I would absolutely recommend it for most types of training.

First of all, I love my Anytime Fitness membership because even out of town on vacation I can visit any local location. This place is great, clean and staffed well. They have a wide variety of machines to choose to work al body parts and a big assortment of cardio equipment too. Kudos!

I have been a member here for over 2 years and I can’t recommend it enough! The owner and ALL of the staff are so friendly and truly go out of their way to give their members a great experience. They have a great variety of machines and dumbbells and are constantly upgrading the gym with updates & new equipment! I love this gym and it has been such a great experience working out here!

name: Monty Ballard YMCA at Cinco Ranch

reviews.text: There are not enough words to describe the truly excellent service that Coach Scott and his team provide for our special kids with our adaptive basketball team. Their patience, kindness, dedication, and outstanding leadership are priceless. I give them 5 stars—and I would give more if it were possible! The service at YMCA Monty Ballard is exceptional; every single person who works there makes this gym a truly unique and wonderful experience. Come and try it out !

The Monty Ballard YMCA has truly become a second home for our family and we’re beyond grateful for the impact it’s had on our son, Kyle.

From the moment he joined the program, we saw something shift in him: more confidence, more joy, and a real passion for the game. And we know that’s largely because of Coach Scott.

Coach Scott isn’t just a coach; he’s a mentor, a role model, and someone who truly cares about the kids he leads. He pours into them with patience, discipline, and encouragement. His words stick with Kyle long after practice ends, and it’s clear he sees coaching as a calling, not just a role.

Thank you to the entire YMCA staff for creating such a positive, safe, and inspiring environment and an especially big THANK YOU to Coach Scott for being such a powerful influence in Kyle’s life. We appreciate you more than words can say.

With gratitude,
Kris Haney (Kyle’s proud parent)

"Five stars for Scott LoMonaco. He is an amazing coach who brings so much energy and passion to the YMCA basketball program. You can tell he truly cares about the kids' development both on and off the court. He pushes them to be better athletes and better people. Thank you, Coach, for your hard work and for being such a positive role model!"

It’s easy for programs to say they’re inclusive, but our experience proved otherwise, until we found the YMCA Adaptive Basketball Team.

My 15-year-old neurodivergent twins, have truly thrived here. They’ve built real friendships, gained confidence, and discovered a love for basketball that keeps them excited week after week.

Coach Scott and his team are incredible. Patient, kind, and genuinely invested in every child’s growth. This program has been life-changing for our family, and we’re so grateful to be part of it.

The facility is nice and the front staff is welcoming, but UPPER MANAGEMENT is HORRIBLE. I’ve emailed, called, left voicemails, even copied the executive director—and after a MONTH, still no response about downgrading my membership. Ignoring members while trapping them in contracts is not honorable way to conduct business, especially for the YMCA, which claims to offer “flexible membership options.” This is the opposite of what they stand for. Please read the reviews before signing up. I would have really liked to call this place a second home for my family but now I cant wait until the annual contract expires to get out from this mess

This place is an absolute joke. You’re genuinely better off getting a normal gym membership those places offer classes, childcare, and don’t play games with your money.

I called to cancel because I haven’t been able to go for months due to foot surgery. Despite that  they still charged me full price today. When I called, I was hit with some nonsense about needing to cancel 10 days before their cutoff date. Funny thing is… that information is nowhere on the online account and you can’t cancel online anyway. You have to call and even then you’re sent to voicemail. I left a message and waited for a callback.

They tried to keep me by offering a “special half off deal,” which I agreed to at first until they called back the same day to tell me I didn’t actually qualify. So I canceled again.

Today I called about a refund for the charge they pulled after I no longer had a membership, and once again I got the same scripted “10-day rule” excuse. Instead of taking responsibility for their shady cancellation process the manager literally hung up on me.

Classy.

Save yourself the frustration. Go to an actual gym that’s cheaper, straightforward, and doesn’t try to squeeze in one last charge on your way out.

We toured the facility for trying to switch from another gym. Monthly membership pricing is reasonable but they really need to renovate and improve the facility. Long dark hallways with very old fixtures. Very few cardio machines had tv and all of them were taken on a saturday afternoon which is a slower day. There is no indoor pool so if kids want to learn swim, thats not an option in the winter. Kids area timings are not very conducive for weekends but the good thing is that kids above a certain age can also workout in the cardio area. The lockers and bathrooms were clean but definitely very outdated and old. Unfortunately we will be looking for another option for our gym needs.

YMCA is a great place to go and workout. Its fun and you deserve a healthier body. The hours are great and clean/safe locker rooms. The employees are always kind and helpful. What else to ask for?....great pool, exercise classes, weight lifting, lots of machine to use and makes you feel great.

Conner was efficient and effective while enrolling my family into the YMCA. He even help me enroll boys into their first swim lessons. Langham Creek YMCA is awesomely beautiful.  The facility is beautiful. The staff is great and all the extracurricular actives are wonderful.

A special thanks to Swim Instructor Kelton, he is super calm and attentive.  Swim Instructor Izzy is over the TOP excellent, she has just enough firmness and patience with the little ones. We love when she's in the swim class with the little ones.

Very nice staff and teachers very helpful for spin class. The gym is well equipped, clean and spacious. The women's locker room is very clean as well. I got a welcome call today for being a new member. Good sense of community here,  excited to use the pool next time I go workout.

name: 24 Hour Fitness
address: 10011 Broadway St Suite 107, Pearland, TX 77584, USA

reviews.text: I just signed up today at the gym after recently moving to Pearland and I had a phenomenal experience. Darrin quickly assisted me as soon as I walked in and made the sign up process smooth and easy to understand. He was extremely professional and answered all my questions while he gave me a tour of the facilities.  He even asked about some of my workout habits and gave personalized tips and how the gym can help me in my fitness goals. Thanks Darrin for going above & beyond in getting me established with the gym. I am so glad I chose to sign up with this gym.

Outstanding Customer Service by Darrin! I had an amazing experience at the 24 Hour Fitness in Pearland. From the moment I walked in, he was professional, friendly, and genuinely attentive. He went above and beyond to answer my questions, made sure I had everything I needed, and even checked back later to ensure I was satisfied. It’s rare to find someone who takes that much pride in their work and truly cares about members’ experience. Darrin represents the gym exceptionally well and his positive attitude and commitment to great service make this location stand out. Highly recommend stopping by and asking for Darrin if you need help!
I have been coming to this location for over 4 years now. The staff is outside and the gym recently had improvements made which made the facility even nicer with more space.

Darrin at 24 Hour Fitness gave my wife and I the best sign up experience we have ever had. He was patient, professional, and took the time to walk us through everything clearly. He made the process simple and made us feel valued from the moment we walked in. Darrin is a true asset to the gym and absolutely deserves a promotion. Highly recommend him.

I have been a member of 24Hour Fitness in Pearland Texas  for many years and was always pleased to see new improvements to the gym. They claim they just had a new remodel but brought in used rusty equipment. I am in the medical field and glad I got my tetanus shot because the treadmills have rust on the hand bars that need to be checked out by the health department. On hot summer days as I enter the gym, I have to pull the gum from under my shoes from the walk up entry into the gym. Never has it been power washed .One day I tried to show the manager, Danielle Lyday the mold that was growing in the drinking fountain. She dismissed me and said she on her way to a conference call.  As I continued to explain, she said the conversation was over and walked away. This is not what I expect in a nice area as Pearland.

Going to home of the Houston Astros, was a blast. The stadium is modern, with a retractable roof and great views from almost every seat. The atmosphere during the game is awesome—fans, music, and cheerleaders really get you into the action. Food and drinks are varied, though a bit pricey, but that doesn’t ruin the experience. Definitely a great spot to hang out with friends or family and enjoy some baseball.

Really nice stadium! The air conditioning makes a big difference compared to the heat outside — way less humid too. It still gets a little warm up in the higher levels, but overall it was super comfortable. Had a great time watching the Astros play here and would definitely come back.
Took a tour of the ballpark during the offseason. The one-hour tour was comprehensive. I was able to visit the press box, the dugouts and walk the field. Two thumbs up!
Came here to see the Astros play the Yankees Labor Day weekend 2023. We were rooting for the Yankees and they won 6-2. Buy parking tickets ahead of time and be prepared to walk.

Don't try to find anything under $20 to drink. Maybe it's been awhile since I've been to a sporting event. But each beverage was 18.99. 2 people alone let alone if you have something to eat too, is easily $100. Plus your tickets. Especially if you have an average job in texas, plan on saving before you go to a game.

If you enjoy baseball, its a beautiful park and can be enclosed on extremely hot days

name: Abshier Stadium
address: 710 W San Augustine St, Deer Park, TX 77536, USA

reviews.text: This is your average football stadium. Definitely needs an upgrade! There isn't enough seating for most games. Ladies, don't use the restroom unless you absolutely have to.

Excellent venue for the UIL Regional HS soccer playoff games. Beautiful stadium with good staff coverage and plenty of security. Video scoreboard is great for watching the action or catching a replay. Everything is well maintained and clean. The field looks great from the stands with alternating shades of green. Plenty of parking for everyone.
Easy to find. Plenty of parking. Large restrooms. And great weather today for youth football!
Went to watch Dobie vs. Seven Lakes state playoff high school game and had a fun time.

Had so much fun watching Deer Park games and seemed like whole community was out supporting their local football team. Great snacks and cold drinks. Local scouts out recruiting. Programs and t-shirts for sale. Come join a great place to watch FOOTBALL FEVER!@

name: Pridgeon Stadium
address: 11355 Falcon Rd a, Houston, TX 77065, USA

reviews.text: Excellent stadium with tons of parking! Clean bathrooms a big bonus. Easily the best concessions I've come across with a wonderful helpful staff. Order the chicken nachos, and you won't be disappointed. The only downside is that the ramps and steps are kinda steep in places and hard to navigate for folks with mobility problems.

Nice stadium. The security inside the stadium is good. They need more patrol outside of the stadium in the parking lot. Bathrooms are usually accessible without too long a wait. They're always clean and well maintained. The lines at the concession stand get ridiculous on big game nights.
First time watching football and the experience was awesome!

I was pretty upset that I went online to purchase tickets ahead of time then found out that visitors have to purchase tickets at the door only. The stadium would not refund my tickets purchase. The website / App Hometown Ticketing should have that information in bold lettering for visitors trying to be proactive in ticket purchasing. Very disappointing.
Easy to park and get inside, nice facility.
At stadium during Rice homecoming.  There were plenty seats. There were many youth presentations but they only got 2 min presentation. Would have liked the group to cheer with cheer team.
An historic stadium, showing its age, aches, and pains, was home to an early Super-bowl, the first in Houston’s illustrious pigskin history.

Site of the 1974 Superbowl this Legendary Stadium has a small crowd due to a small alumni and a small enrollment at Rice University.  The stadium has great views but does lack those plastic seats which are primarily in the upper rows under cover of the upper deck.  Overall the price is an unbeatable bargain for Division 1 conference USA football.

This is one of the nicest stadiums I’ve ever been to. I went to watch the Westlake v. North Shore semifinals, and had a very pleasant experience. It was very well kept, and the seating was very free and open. I’m so used to being cramped and uncomfortable in other more popular stadiums such as DKR, but this was way more laid back and frankly a breath of fresh air as far as stadiums go. Will definitely return!

The stadium has a lot of history and is nostalgic. Restrooms are not very modernize. Kind of throwback to the sixties. But the field was very nice. And the stadium seats where your typical aluminum seats bench seats. Great time, easy parking, small crowd!
Beautiful staduim, clean restrooms great atmosphere home of the mustangs!
You can't even bring your own clear water bottle. No senior citizen discounts if you are a visitor. And the staff, we'll they're rude.

Small stadium for one of our biggest games ever. The field literally has a curve in it. The concessions took so long and by the time we got there they were out of everything even though we got there early before everybody else.
Very very clean and very very spacious stadium. Huge parking lots, huge handicap accessible area, plenty of concession options.
All love this stadium, it's a great place to watch Galena Park and North Shore high school football games ..

Yes once again Young Women College Prep Academy takes 1st Place in HISD 4A Volleyball but this time in dramatic fashion vs Wheatley at Barnett Sports Complex. Hats off to this all girls school located in Third Ward. Third straight district title in last 4 years. They have only had a volleyball program for 6 years! Barnett has good viewing seats and clean facilities. Old and rich with history. My only gripe is location! Back off in the neighborhood with potholes leading to the complex and not an easy entry and exit! Not the best place to be hanging out late night. Plenty of HISD police security on sight though and normally like this evening there is a football contest going on which adds to the traffic.

This is a great facility that rivals most of the new expensive stadiums.  It is  a solid structure that holds twice as much spectators and very accommodating for the athletes.  The only issue seems to be the keep ups and maintenance!!
HISD Get your act together!!
Restrooms are decent, it’s a nice stadium overall, pretty basic, fairly clean. Gets packed quickly, but there’s a good amount of seats

Only problem I found is when u buy your ticket,it can only be used the one time,so if you leave for any reason you will have to purchase another ticket....so either make sure you bring plenty of money or just make sure that you don't forget anything before going in,it's an awesome stadium,very nice!!! Had such an amazing sun set during my son's football game,he is a senior this year at Splendora ISD,when he graduates he is going to be a Marine for the USA,I'm so proud of him and so thankful to God for him to be blessed enough to play at so many wonderful locations especially as beautiful as Barnett Sports complex thank u for allowing my son the experience!!!! Thank u for having us!!!!
Love It They Just Need To Get That Road Leading Into The Stadium Repaved It's A Total Nightmare

name: Constellation Field
address: 1 Stadium Dr, Sugar Land, TX 77498, USA

reviews.text: Amazing lights display!!!  Comfortable to walk around in.  Whether you're walking the concourse checking out the vendors or down on the field ice skating, it's a wonderful venue!!!  Stop and say Hi!! to Santa!!!

Good stadium with atmosphere and space for the family to have fun. It’s well set up for young families. Parking isn’t ideal as I came on time but had to pay to park on a grass area. They should expand the parking. Food options were standard fried baseball stuff. Overall was fun and ticket prices were reasonable.
Sugar Land Holiday Lights was great. Over 3 million lights to ohh and ahh at. I recommend you see it in person.

Great stadium. A lot of really cool areas, all with great views. Plenty to distract you & your kids as well.
My thanks to Caira & Anna in the team store going the extra mile to help me out.
The CF to LF seats along the OF fence stand out as unique.

First time here. Had a great time watching the Aviators vs the Space Cowboys game on Sunday. I enjoyed their hot dogs. The gift shop has a nice variety of different things to support both the Houston Astros and the Space Cowboys teams. I recommend this place for a family outing. Great atmosphere.

Nice stadium, great for big games with crowd participation.  Only issue I've experienced is when trying to purchase tickets online it never works.  If they don't let me in for free I have to leave because they don't take cash nor do they have attendants to assist with debit/cc payments.

We loved our experience at Rhodes Stadium. It is spacious and modern. It had big nice screens that helped us keep track of the game and score. Well equipped and prepared with police officers and ambulance. The concession stand had options I haven't seen in other places; for example burgers.

Rhodes Stadium has a clean and reputable stadium interior, as well as the exterior outside the stadium entrance gate. Onsite parking is very spacious and routed well for traffic once inside the complex. Security measures were tight, but one of the main reasons for the added value here in the Houston area. A very impressive piece of school property! Thank you very much for the opportunity we received!!!!!

It’s very clean and I truly enjoyed that the benches were closer than most football fields. Made watching the game more interesting. I didn’t like that they didn’t have signs for the entrance the visitors or home. They have four entrances so we saw one with the home team colors and we just assumed the other was our entrance. They didn’t let us through and we had to get our vehicle and park again so we could enter on our team’s side. They just need a sign or something saying visitor entrance or home team entrance.
Beautiful stadium. Perfect setting for Texas football! Nice concession stand with the perfect variety of food and snacks to watch during a game. Seating is ample and not a bad view in the stadium.

This was a great stadium! It had good concessions and clean bathrooms. The staff seemed to be nice. The sound and video screen were sharp. Parking was a dream compared to many of the other high school stadiums. Plenty of parking and avenues for entry and exit.

The only down side was the ramp up to the stands and in part the bleachers were steep and took just a little more effort to climb up. It would be difficult for a person in a wheelchair or on crutches to navigate the ramp without someone to help.
Older basic stadium that serves its purpose. No frills but plenty of parking.

We enjoyed  watching a great game in this huge stadium.  This is an older stadium and it might be time for a good power wash.  However, this is a very solid and safe building and they keep it very clean.
Went to a high school football game. Good views all around. Parking was good

We have had a great time watching our sons games here. Will forever have really good memories however they really need to monitor the student much better. Lots of illegal activity going on in the restrooms.  Witnessed drug transactions and lots of unwanted loitering in the restrooms while people are trying to use the restroom.

name: Don Coleman Coliseum
address: 1050 Dairy Ashford Rd, Houston, TX 77079, USA

reviews.text:
I’ve seen numerous playoff basketball and volleyball series here (usually the Region tournament), and while the arena is simply laid out and not particularly new, it’s very well cared for and staffed appropriately for the crowd sizes.
The horizontal slope of the seating means that there isn’t really a bad seat in the house, and I love how the arena floor is the low point in the bowl. It can make the action on the court seem even bigger, faster and more impactful.
If you get a chance to see a big-time matchup here or attend a tournament, I recommend it. First-class people and first-class facility.
Nice facility. Came for the Houston 2024 UMA Taekwondo tournament. It was chaotic compared with the same event last year.
It was my good friend My Everything friend everything lady son graduation
Nice facility  great for high school.
Finding your seat is a nightmare since they only have row #s and no aisle #

name: Edward Mercer Stadium
address: 16403 Lexington Blvd, Sugar Land, TX 77479, USA

reviews.text: Edward Mercer Stadium is a great place to catch a game or event. The parking is well-organized and easy to access, which makes arriving and leaving stress-free. The seating is comfortable with good views from almost anywhere in the stadium. The food concessions are also impressive, offering a nice variety of options at reasonable prices. Overall, it’s a clean, convenient, and enjoyable stadium experience.
This is one of our home football stadium for FBISD! Conveniently located. Lots of parking if not a sell out game, locker rooms okay but needs upgrades with larger showers 🚿 and restrooms!

Sound system is extremely loud on the home side for no reason.

Different workers each week with different expectations and reasoning.

Some gates open one week and not the next.

Extra large security presence one week and mininlmal the next.

For as large as FBISD is, this stadium has fallen behind many other similar districts and smaller ones in the area.

It was a lot of walking up and down hills, and we had to park across the street in the Sugar Land Mall parking lot, but it was all worth it to see my two Princess Granddaughters walk across that stage.
The parking lot of the stadium was easy to find. The tickets were $8 cash for EVERYONE. The concession stand was in plain view as you walked through the gate and the stadium was nice and clean.

name: Leonard E. Merrell Center
address: 6301 S Stadium Ln, Katy, TX 77494, USA

reviews.text: I recently visited the Leonard E. Merrell Center in Katy, Texas, for the Katy Community Expo on August 25, 2024. This was my first time at the venue, and I was impressed with its spacious and well-organized layout. The facility was clean and well-maintained, providing a comfortable environment for the event. The staff were friendly and helpful, and the expo itself was a great showcase of local businesses and community services. Overall, it was a positive experience, and I would recommend the Merrell Center for future events.

Really impressed with the facility!  Wonder if it's ever used for concerts or other events? Aware of the Rice festival held outside but anyways had a great time during the art awards! Serious talent being nurtured in KISD!

Foster high school had their senior graduation at the Merrel Center it is a great venue for big events, A/C keeps the building cool and the graduation happened in a very organized manner. Set-up team did a great job! I enjoy the way the hallways and main floor are build it is a great venue for big events. We have great memories of our son’s graduation!

It’s good, but also really small. We didn’t really have enough space to move around. I enjoyed all the events that were hosted here. There was a leadership conference in the center of the stadium when I was there. The place is also really clean considering how much people has been there.

It was a little warm inside but it was very nice. We went for the Spring Fest Holiday Market, and they had great vendors and tons of different gems to discover. There were a lot of outdoor decor items and some great Spring and Summer attire. Not to mention, they had special guest star Melanie Kohn, the Voice of Lucy Van Pelt from Peanuts! Oh, and I have to mention Mr. Cedric, the "Praline King" Minor. He's got those melt in your mouth Pralines, and there's an awesome "spicy" flavor! If you haven't been, do plan to attend next time and bring a friend or two. You will not be disappointed!!

name: Woodforest Bank Stadium
address: 19115 David Memorial Dr, Shenandoah, TX 77385, USA

reviews.text:
I went up here with my lady to help cheer on her son, whose team was playing in the playoffs on a cool Friday night high school football game.

It's a pretty nice suburban stadium with an impressive scoreboard.

This is probably the norm here in Texas, as where I grew up in the inner city watching public league football, the budgets were miniscule compared to the suburbs.

You had typical long aluminum benches to sit on, and I noticed some fans sitting on these custom made seats that looked so comfortable and peaked my interest.

All in all, a good time though my partners sons team came out on the losing end.

What a great stadium and atmosphere for a cool Friday night of football. Very clean very well organized and the police have it under control. Parking was a fast in and out. Make sure to purchase online tickets and do not bring backpacks that are not clear transparent.
Highly recommended for your High school games.
Another great Stadium on our 2024 tour!  Nice stadium, tons of parking close to the entrance, great concessions as well.  The big screen was fantastic, and sound system was clear & LOUD!
Bathrooms were clean,  sound was good,  nice facility.  4 stars, not 5, no one to direct traffic out of the stadium.

Beautiful stadium. Fantastic access for the band for load in and load out. Staff was very kind. The only issue was getting out of the parking lot. There are no signs or directions so it looks like you can get out and then it'll be blocked very confusing.

Parking is the main issue here.

Sound system is good. Plenty of nice and competent staff willing to help.

Areas in and around were clean.

Keep it up Friendswood

Very nice! Very clean and new! Concession stand had good food and snacks and a variety to choose from. They even sell chick filet sandwiches for 5 dollars. My son who is in 10th grade played the home team, we were visitors. 2nd quarter my son was hit hard and got a concussion. I am so thankful to their high school football staff and trainers for all their help making sure my boy was ok. Beside my son getting injured we had a nice visit at Henry Winston Stadium.  BTW, MY SON IS OK!!! THANK THE LORD!!!🤕☺🏥🏈🙋🖒💯
Very nice facilities & turf field for our high school soccer game!

Great place for a High School Football Game
Please remember to hit the like button if my review helped you in anyway.
Limited parking and a loong walk for the visitors. Consession folks were super nice. Great marching Band! Cool fly over too!
What an amazing place to play ball. Parking can be a bit strange but just depends on game time. The stands themselves are super comfortable with real stadium seating, and not bleachers.
Absolutely love this place. Got to watch a Coogs baseball game from the suite. Clean, well kept and very nice staff. I'll go back every chance I get.
Great baseball field.  Everything seemed new and kept up.  Seats were as comfortable as it gets for an outdoors stadium.

Great Location for a Baseball Park. On the edge of campus with ample parking ($10 as you enter) across the street. All the seats have great views, the concessions are pretty standard but they usually have a food truck or two out in front. Enjoy the game and cheer for the home team!

Great place to view baseball. All seats have good line of sight.
Food is not pro team prices.
Plenty of shade for fans.

The stadium was really fun for kids night! No matter where you sit, it’s a good seat. Loved the high energy. It lost a star because coming from Corpus Christi - my son and I Ubered to the game leaving my truck at the hotel. Immediately upon arrival, we went to game store to purchase some jerseys for us to wear along with a soccer ball. When we purchased this soccer ball at no time did the store tell us, “if you’re going to the game make sure it’s deflated.” We walked out, stood in line for security, and wasn’t allowed in. We were told, we had to get rid of the ball because it was inflated. Well as a spectator coming from out of town with no car to go secure the ball in, that was kind of hard to achieve! The break in communication from the team store was really frustrating! Either hang a sign or train cashiers to deflate the balls. I appreciate a security supervisor escorting us to guest services to turn in the ball until after the game. He made an exception for us and knowing that, I could’ve been out $40 on a ball by just leaving it outside of the stadium just to gain entry for the game. This NEEDS to be reevaluated. Otherwise, my son and I had a great time!

Nestled in the heart of Houston, the Dynamo Soccer Club Stadium stands as a testament to the city's love for the beautiful game. From the moment you approach the stadium, its sleek, modern architecture catches your eye, drawing you in with its promise of an unforgettable sporting experience.

As you step inside, you're greeted by a sea of orange and blue – the team colors of the Houston Dynamo – as fans from all walks of life come together to cheer on their beloved club. The energy in the air is palpable, crackling with excitement and anticipation for the match ahead.

The stadium itself is a marvel of engineering, with state-of-the-art facilities and amenities that cater to every need. Whether you're grabbing a bite to eat at one of the concession stands, perusing the team merchandise at the souvenir shop, or simply taking in the sights and sounds of the bustling concourse, there's something for everyone to enjoy.

But it's when the players take to the field that the magic truly happens. The roar of the crowd, the thud of the ball against the turf, the sheer athleticism on display – it's a spectacle that's impossible to forget. And as the game unfolds, you find yourself swept up in the drama and excitement, cheering on your team with all your heart.

But the Dynamo Soccer Club is more than just a stadium – it's a community. It's the camaraderie shared between fans, the passion for the game that unites us all, and the memories made with friends and family that last a lifetime. Whether you're a die-hard supporter or a casual observer, there's no denying the special bond that exists within these walls.

So if you ever find yourself in Houston, do yourself a favor and pay a visit to the Dynamo Soccer Club Stadium. Whether you're a soccer fan or not, you're sure to be swept away by the electric atmosphere and the sheer joy of the beautiful game. It's an experience you won't soon forget.

i suggest parking next to the stadium ($50) but well ran security to prevent break ins

The cheapest ticket in town as far as professional sports in Houston is concerned. A very fun place if you're a soccer fan like me - I'm happy to be part of the supporter's group who chant all game long for our boys in orange! They now have one of the best food verities in any sport with Vietnamese sandwiches, great burger shop, Mexican food and bar b q!
Nice stadium, missed the 1st half after being stuck on I-10  and finding parking !
What a sad demise of what was a great club. A friend and season ticket holder said it right, " We should have all left when Dominic Kinnear quit. He knew it was going to get bad."
A lot of soccer fields installed with the ability to play baseball on them.All in all, it is a great place for youth soccer and baseball.

Why is this stadium rarely used?  Why is it other sports can’t have access to the stadium.  It’s locked up constantly.  Many non uil sports would be happy to pay to use it and it’s forbidden.  It literally just sits there unused most of the year… shame.
Very nice little stadium...restrooms on both sides and a concession stand.

There are no signs showing where the entrances and exits are located. The bleacher seating is okay, but there is NO SHADE! For 5:00pm games, the sun shines directly in your eyes if your are the home team.
Great parking, stands have bench seating, close to the field. Concessions usually available. Restrooms clean.

The parking is horrendous. Navigating to the stands is a bit of a hassle. The press box is filthy, especially the windows and the bathroom. I have never seen such a lack of upkeep on a facility before. The seats are unclean as well in the stands. Overall, this facility is filthy and needs to be cleaned. It honestly looks as if no one has cleaned the place in years.
Parking is horrible. There were no staff helping people get in this stadium. Just poorly ran.

We walked by the visitors' entrance. None of the workers told me my purse was too big for entry. It was also not mentioned on the website when I purchased the ticket. It was a long walk there and back to car that could have simply been avoided.

The layout for unloading the band and drill teams is on the far side of the stadium. First time going to the stadium is a trick to finding the correct parking lot. Cash only concessions but online ticketing only.

Overall nice facility but need better signage on where to park. Had not been here before and ended up parking at the school thinking it was all connected.  I had to go back to my car, get back on the feeder, go all the way down to the next exit and get back in he traffic I had just gotten out of to find my way back up the feeder and to the proper entrance. Ughhh

name: Pearland ISD Stadium
address: 3775 S Main St, Pearland, TX 77581, USA

reviews.text: Was impressed with this stadium! Comfortable seating... no more cold metal bleachers! Accessibility options were plentiful. Stadium was clean and parking was good.

field was in good shape
parking was ok not the best noticed when leaving several car having to park in grass area. does appear the parking would be good for a large event
as far as the sound it may be ok on home side but on vistors we got to hear the announcer 3 or 4 times it echoes terrible very hard to understand ps this does matter to both Dawson and Pearland since they share the stadium they have to be visitor at some point.

Well maintained sports facilities considering that it's just a high school. I personally was impressed with the environment and would recommend that one checks their academic performance too so as to get a well-rounded perspective.
