Huge stadium! Bathrooms kept tidy. Attended a track meet here and was very impressed with my surroundings. Great first impression!!

Son had a track meet here on May 15. Nice stadium and clean. Track meet was well run.  Had food trucks as well.  We brought a pop up canopy and had plenty of space. Nice track overall, would be happy to go back.

It was our first time staying at this beautifully restored hotel that used to be a historic bank, and what a gorgeous place. The décor was already set up for the holidays and looked absolutely stunning!

We decided to have dinner at the bar, even though the restaurant had plenty of space. The service was friendly, sharing the stories of ghosts haunting the place. The best part was the food – super impressive. We shared a margarita pizza to start, then had the Thai salad with shrimp and the Greek salad with steak. Everything was full of flavor, but the real standout was the dressing: garlicky, bright, and unforgettable.

Hands-down this was an exceptional experience. When we are back on Houston – we will stay there again.

My experience at ICON was probably average for an overnight stay. The hotel common areas are neat and clean. The rooms were clean and have the expected amenities. Did not look for WiFi, but television could have greater offerings. Parking valets can seem a little aggressive at first. Food (dinner, breakfast buffet) was good but fairly standard fare, with the buffet included in the reservation. The room views are buildings - what one might expect in downtown Houston. Cigarette butts and the lingering smell of smoke around the entrance took away from the first impression. I would stay here again if the situation demanded it (business, but not leisure).

What an amazing place to stay located right in the heart of downtown Houston. Staff were amazing and very accommodating. Valet service members were exceptionally professional and courteous. Definitely will be back. Beautiful architectural landmark. Once and old bank over 100 year old legendary iconic building. We were able to check in early and check out late. Best place we’ve ever stayed !!!

Beautiful room. Some boutique rooms can be small but not here. Set up a romantic staycation with wife. Staff very friendly. Lovely bar area when you check in. Now....the elevators are small. I think it's because the building originally was a bank. Decor reminds me of the 1920s. Rooms are soundproof with blackout curtains. Bed has an outstanding weighted blanket. Haven't tried food service yet. Will definitely return.

Outstanding Hotel!! This renovated building that once was a bank, is Stunning!! I loved the old vault, my massive rooms, and the elevator with the old fashion “level meter.”  all Outstanding!!! The staff were simply Fantastic!!! I would rate this hotel a full 10 stars if I could!!!

name: Magnolia Hotel Houston, a Tribute Portfolio Hotel

reviews.text:
I stayed at The Magnolia Houston for four nights (January 19–23, 2026) and had a good overall experience.

The room was clean and comfortable, though I did find a discarded press-on nail on the floor one evening, which was a bit surprising. Aside from that, housekeeping was solid. WiFi was reliable, and the bathroom and shower were very good — great water pressure and well maintained.

The on-site gym is about average. It’s fine for a basic workout, but the treadmills aren’t great quality. The rooftop pool is nice but fairly small, so don’t expect a resort-style setup.

Front desk and bell service were excellent — friendly, helpful, and professional throughout my stay. One of the real highlights of the hotel is the second-floor restaurant, The Dispatch. The food quality and selection were excellent. I really enjoyed the breakfast buffet, as well as the lunch and dinner options.

The hotel is conveniently located downtown, close to the convention centre and other attractions. That said, one block over near the metro line can feel a bit sketchy, and you do need to stay alert when walking around, especially in the evening.

The hotel itself was quiet, not overly crowded, and comfortable — which I appreciated. Overall, I would recommend staying here, particularly for business travel or convention visits, and I’d happily stay again.

The staff at this hotel get 5 stars! Everyone from the welcome desk to the dispatch restaurant have been great! The shuttle really made a difference in our trip since we were here for a convention! Special shout out to Marcayla A. at the dispatch who made sure our morning were started right. It’s a tribute hotel so obviously the interiors are a little “aged” but still classy as always

I went to a concert at Daikin Park this weekend and stayed at The Magnolia with my daughter as it was close to Daikin Park.The hotel is nice and clean, courteous; however, the hot water in the shower was really hard to get and the tub was very high and slippery, there is really not much to do downtown on the weekend as everything is closed, there are no little stores in the hotel or around to purchase snacks, water, anything.  The hotel was close to the venue but it rained really hard and instead of walking we had to get an Uber for 5 blocks but that was Mother Nature.  We were able to walk back to the hotel after the concert.  That was good.  Downtown really has nothing to offer close to this hotel on the weekend. The tunnels are closed and not many choices for restaurants. The hotel did offer cookies and milk in the lobby at 8 pm and they were delicious.  Thank you.

The hotel was clean and the staff was friendly. I liked the layout of our room. It didn’t feel like a typical hotel room to me. There was a lot of character to the room. The AC didn’t get cold at all so the stay wasn’t very comfortable. The pool area was really cute and the view up there was nice. It was great walking distance from Daikin Park.

I will start by saying the staff was friendly upon our arrival. However, the rooms are outdated seemed very moldy need renovations and was not disclosed that there was a $58 charge for valet. Check my account and they charged me twice for valet and I was there for only one night. It’s not about the $58. It’s about transparency and to add insult to injury they charge me twice for valet and said oh our fault my bad please do better. I will never stay at this hotel again.

We had an absolutely wonderful stay at The Lancaster Hotel, and what truly sets this place apart is the service. From the moment we arrived, every single staff member went above and beyond, attentive, genuinely kind, and incredibly professional. This is the kind of service that anticipates your needs and makes you feel truly cared for, not just checked in.
The hotel itself is elegant, comfortable, and beautifully maintained, but the experience is elevated even further by Cultivated F+B, located inside the hotel. The food was outstanding from start to finish, thoughtfully prepared, fresh, and full of flavor. The deviled eggs were a standout, and everything we tried reflected a level of care and quality that matched the hotel’s service perfectly.
If you’re looking for a boutique hotel in Houston in the Theater Districr where hospitality genuinely goes the extra mile and the dining is just as memorable, The Lancaster Hotel delivers on every level. We’ll absolutely be back. We had a great stay-cation!

Very attentive staff from start to finish. The staff will go over and above to satisfy almost any request. Wife wanted garlic fries at 1030 on a Friday night. I call the kitchen they say yah we will give it shot, it wasn't on the menu. Room service arrived within 10 minutes (keep in mind 12th floor) beautiful well pomme frites garlicky shaved warmest. Well done!! Rooms are well appointed, location is sublime. The service though is the most enviable aspect in my opinion.

A Truly World-Class Experience at The Lancaster Hotel
Our recent stay at the Lancaster Hotel was nothing short of exceptional. From the moment we stepped inside, we were enveloped in an atmosphere of timeless elegance, refined taste, and genuine high-class hospitality. This is not just a hotel—it is a masterclass in what true luxury and world-class service should look like.
We were deeply impressed by the thoughtful welcome amenities: a beautifully chilled bottle of champagne, perfectly prepared chocolate-covered strawberries, and, above all, the handwritten note from the General Manager himself. That personal touch transformed a simple arrival into a warm, memorable moment—something very few hotels bother to do anymore, yet it makes all the difference.
Every interaction with the staff was flawless. Professional, attentive, and sincerely kind, they made us feel valued from start to finish. The level of courtesy and care at The Lancaster sets a new standard—one that every hotel should study and strive to emulate. This is precisely what world-class hospitality means.
The ambiance, décor, comfort, and overall atmosphere exude sophistication without pretension. It is clear that the team behind The Lancaster takes immense pride in offering an unparalleled experience to its guests.
We left with the feeling that we had been truly cared for, and we will absolutely return. In a world full of hotels that claim to be “luxury,” The Lancaster is one of the rare few that actually delivers—and elevates the definition of excellence.

Highly, wholeheartedly recommended.

We came to Houston and Lancaster Hotel for the first time to see Christmas performance by a favored group of ours Voctave. We have really enjoyed the hotel. Bartender David knows his stuff. Restaurant was great. Breakfast was of great quality and the new gal on staff from Rhode Island made us feel welcome. As did Gary and Kevin at the front desk. Very helpful. Housekeeping and guys at the front door was prompt and friendly. Finally the manager, I didn’t catch his name either. He never stopped working! Always on the move. Setting a great example for staff. Thank you for great service and making us feel welcome.

Top-notch,  outstanding five stars across the board!
Every single detail was thought fully curated. The bar was low-key simply fabulous!
The artwork throughout the hotel is just frosting on the cake !  We enjoyed our stay and will definitely be back!

name: Courtyard by Marriott Houston Downtown/Convention Center

reviews.text:
Overall, great hotel. It was clean and my king suite was spacious. It's kind with 2 separate rooms.  There was plenty of space and lots of counter space, drawers and cabinets.
The location is interesting.  It's a shared space/block with 2 other Marriott hotels,  so the outside valet/checkin area can get crowded. Only valet is offered.
Only issue I had was with my late check out. I got back to my room,  and my key didn't work.  Ughhh! Not that big of a deal except I had things in my hand and my room was on the 7th floor at the END of the hall.
Also note, it is an older hotel so there were only 2 USB ports in the living area,  so you HAVE to have a charger base to plug in anywhere else and there's no blow dryer in the room.

I would like to recognize Barbara Ruiz for the exceptional service she did provide for me during a very tough time and when we needed the most. Her expertise and knowledge is admirable. The time she spent to make sure I met my needs and her support meant a lot to us. She is a great asset for Marriott and me and our friends will continue to stay at this great hotel anywhere we go and specially in Huston , Tx .
Sepi Hamadani

I dont usually review hotels as they are usually average and meet the expectations of the price point. However these ladies have been wonderful. I was already traveling before I came here so I didn't have a fan but they brought me a new clean box fan for my stay. I get rooms with two beds because I like to have lots of pillows and extra blankets. Instead of making both beds everyday, they added all the extra pillows, sheets, and blankets to the bed I was using. The lady at the bar took the time in her busy evening time to see my water bottle needed ice and brought me some and let me know when the best times were to buy and what to get for food and drink. These sound small but they make a HUGE difference when you feel seen and not just a guest number.

TL;DR:
Avoid this hotel and its adjacent, connecting properties. Sketchy staff/security, bed bugs, and dismissive, apathetic management.

Full Review:
I rarely write negative reviews, but this one is necessary. I hope it deters others from booking here and spares you a similar experience.

I booked this hotel for my stay during the AfroTech conference in Houston, checking in on October 27th.

On the evening of October 29th, I returned to find myself hard-locked out of my room by staff. Security later told me it was an “accident” — they “meant to lock a different room ending in 9.” Anyone who’s ever stayed in a hotel knows how room numbering works: there isn’t “another room ending in 9” near mine. This kind of carelessness is unacceptable. I lost my evening plans resolving this with staff and security just to regain access to a room I had paid for.

Then, early morning on October 30th around 4:30 AM, I woke up to something crawling on me — a bed bug. I immediately called the front desk and asked to be moved to a different, non-adjacent hotel. The manager later offered to move me to SpringHill Suites or Residence Inn, both of which are connected to this same building. Anyone who understands pest control knows that infestations are not contained to one section of a shared structure — which is why I refused and requested a transfer to a separate property.

The front desk told me only the manager could authorize this and that she wouldn’t be in until 8 AM. I spent the next several hours inspecting and sealing my belongings. By 8:45 AM, still no update. When I called again, I was told the most they could do was waive the early checkout fee and have pest control inspect the room.

While explaining both the hard lock and the bed bug incidents to the manager, she repeatedly cut me off and, at one point, said verbatim: “It sounds like you’re reading from a script.” That comment was condescending and completely inappropriate. I was simply recounting events clearly and calmly.

As a Marriott Gold Elite member, I typically choose Marriott for its reliability and professionalism — but this location and its management are a poor reflection of the brand.

This isn’t about money — my trip was sponsored. It’s about basic respect, safety, and accountability.

To corporate: please handle this situation with the seriousness it deserves.
And to management: please refrain from posting a canned apology. It only adds insult to injury.

Very close to a lot of things, ALL staff is friendly, hospitable and welcoming. Very accommodating, rooms are clean, has Starbucks in the lobby. For my first stay in Houston I would def book again. I would advise that they have $100 hold per night just so future/potential guest are aware.

name: Residence Inn by Marriott Houston Downtown/Convention Center

reviews.text:
Update: I got a call from Hattie but when I called back she was unavailable so I spoke with Brian on my issues. I’m really pleased with the way they handled my concerns and issues. I hated to leave a bad review on this company in general because it is my favorite stay away from home but, again that’s why I choose Marriott because their customer service remains top tier and the staff made me feel heard and I appreciate that most guys! Thanks so much and I appreciate you all so much.

I’m so disappointed and disgusted with this Hotel! I did reach out to corporate to voice all my concerns and issues. I really think this particular hotel needs to remember that Customer SERVICE IS EVERYTHING! On top of that the hotel needs an MAJOR UPGRADE Especially when my daughters and I just stayed at another Residence Inn this September for 3 to 4 days and it was the best vacation we ever had. Galveston location is TOP TIER HOTEL AND CUSTOMER SERVICE. Like it’s unbelievable how much I hate this stay. Corporate will be resolving this issue immediately or I’ll go to BBB! I’m not joking in no kind of way!

Customer service was beyond outstanding. Right in the middle of downtown but very accessible to so many venues within walking distance. A big plus, full kitchen in the room plus a great morning complimentary breakfast before you head out.$$$ Definitely recommend!

Rav and his staff were great hosts for my 1 week stay. Comfortable clean room, good variety at breakfast. Hotel conveniently located near light rail and major bus stops, easy walks to theater district and central downtown.

It's a nice place to stay while attending a conference.  The rooms are a nice size with a full kitchen.  Nice restaurants within walking distance.  Everyone on the staff are amazing and willing to go above and beyond to ensure satisfaction.

The hotel is located right on the main road, making it very convenient to access. The rooms are spacious, clean, and well maintained. The service was excellent, and the overall vibe of the place was really good. Highly recommended for a comfortable stay.

The hotel's location is its biggest advantage - right in downtown Houston, walking distance to the convention center, major offices, and plenty of good restaurants. The restaurant inside the property is also convenient for quick meals or meetings.
Unfortunately, the overall experience didn't match expectations. The front desk staff was dismissive and unhelpful when I asked about cleaning service and a few basic questions. The room itself felt dated and poorly maintained - the fridge had black spots that looked like mold, which was unacceptable for a hotel in this price range. The housekeeping service was inconsistent, and common areas didn't feel particularly clean or welcoming

I liked the hotel overall and it does its job well. The service is good and the staff is friendly. A very convenient plus is the free coffee and water.
The location is great if you’re visiting downtown Houston, as it’s very close to everything.
The only downside is that it can be a bit noisy — you can hear ambulances and the train, so it might be an issue if you’re a light sleeper.
Overall, it’s a good option because of the location and service, as long as you don’t mind some noise.

Motel in a 4-star shell.
Rooms were dated and dusty and some of the bedding was stained but at least it was bug-free.
External sound-proofing was great, we couldn't hear the guy shouting in the street once we entered the hotel. Internal sound-proofing could use some work, we could clearly hear everything going on in the room next to us.
The provided coffee in our rooms came with a single serving (for a room for 2) the day we checked-in and was never refilled throughout our stay, but at least the coffee machine in the lobby was decent.
The check-in kiosks were handy and easy to use, which was great because the staff we interacted with were mostly uncommunicative and unhelpful.

Overall not the worst place to stay if the prices are motel low but lower your expectations accordingly.

I travel for work to Houston often and had never heard of this hotel. I walk to my office and needed something close. I am so glad I made this choice, every staff member was kind and helpful which I find rare. The rooms are small but I travel alone and dont need extra space, I do need places to work not sitting in my room and there were plenty of locations and all comfortable. They were accommodating, had a yoga session daily on the TV. I felt safe with locked doors and kind attentive valets. Noise level was nonexistent to outside and I was concerned with the closeness of rooms but heard nothing from either side. The restaurant attached had a burger I talked about for two days! So good. This will be my go to hotel when im in Houston for work.

This place was cute. Checked in on a tablet and made my own room keys. How fun.. rooms are quite tiny but are quiet. Water bottle stations in the halls to fill up free water. Nice touch there. Would prefer having single use items like the soaps and shampoo vs a community bottle. Thats not sanitary and very unappealing. The carpet in the rooms is not clean so wear slippers or socks. The bed skirt in our room was also really stained but since I dont sleep on that its just visually gross. Overall its ok.

I have been coming to Houston for years on business, and it is regrettable that I am only now discovering The Whitehall Houston. This is a fantastic establishment, through and through—from beginning to end, and even beyond. From the moment I checked in, I was greeted with warm hospitality and delightful conversation. I am especially thankful for the front desk team—Shirley, John, Ana Maria, and Kelvin—who made sure I was settled into a wonderful, spacious room and handled all of my needs throughout my entire stay with utmost care.

I was in Houston on business and had many Amazon packages arriving during my stay. The team made sure to notify me promptly and took great care to ensure everything reached me in a timely manner. Even with a return package that needed to make its way back to Amazon after my departure, they were fully committed to helping. All of my business needs were addressed—from printing and signing legal agreements to faxing them as well. This level of service is truly over the top.

The entire staff was friendly and incredibly helpful, offering recommendations for navigating downtown Houston, and their team even served as personal chauffeurs using the hotel car. And let’s not forget the on-property restaurant, Edgars. The food is fresh, delicious, and inventive—a thoughtful fusion of Texan, Mexican, and Southern comfort flavors—paired with care and attention given to every beverage they offer.

This is a wonderful place to stay, and I plan to stay here again whenever I am in Houston!

A quiet hotel with business travelers. Good for rest and central location. Cooked to order breakfast vouchers are a nice alternative to traditional continental breakfast options. Large rooms with great light. The spiral staircase and literal white hall are special touches that complete the stay! Shirley and Kelvin were very nice accommodating with an early check in. Much appreciated!

Beautiful hotel and a genuine standout. The experience felt like thoughtfully executed luxury rather than performative polish.

Food was excellent, the coffee memorable, and the staff impressively informed. They spoke with ease about the building’s history, the nuances of the stay, and even remembered my name, a detail that signals real hospitality rather than training.

The room itself was immaculate and generously sized. Temperature control was precise, no small feat in Houston, as anyone local knows. The deep soaking garden tub connected to the room was an unexpected pleasure and elevated the stay considerably.

My partner remarked that they had not slept so well or felt so restored in years, which was the entire purpose of the trip and the clearest measure of success. Some street noise does carry through during moments when emergency vehicles pass with audible alerts, but it remains well below the levels I have experienced at comparable hotels.

Taken as a whole, The Whitehall earns an 8.6 out of 10 for a three day weekend stay and delivers exactly what it promises: comfort, competence, and a sense of care.

First time staying at the White hall I was very impressed. The rims are very comfortable. The hotel is very clean. The staff was really great. Upon check-in Shirley and Kelvin didn’t an amazing job getting me a beautiful room. I enjoy having some drinks and food at the Inn hotel restaurant and hotel is in walking distance to many other great restaurants in the area. District 7 restaurant has a great breakfast and isn’t walking distance. I enjoyed that… next time. I’m in town. I’ll definitely be stopping at the Whitehall..

The Whitehall Hotel is the best hotel that I have stayed at. It is very clean and beautiful. When my daughter and I came in the hotel, Shirley and John was so nice and helpful. We were both greeted when we checked in. They are both so awesome and the best I have ever seen. And I seen them again, and they asked if our stay is good, and they are so good with customers. The hotel is very beautiful when you come inside and outside. It is a great place to stay. I will be staying at The Whitehall again from now on.

I have stayed here a couple times. My first time staying here I stayed for two weeks. Didn’t want to leave. The service is great. Room service available, pool & gym. The concierge is top notch. They can handle car rentals so you don’t have worry about going through an app like Toro or have the unpredictability of car rentals at the airport. They can also arrange a driver to pick you up at the airport. Super convenient. The hotel is relatively close to the airport as well. Cleanliness is a big thing for me. And the housekeeping at four seasons especially this one is top notch. Definitely would stay here again.

I just finished a trip in which I stayed at three 5-star hotels back-to-back, including Four Seasons Houston, along with Auberge Bowie in Fort Worth and Ritz-Carlton in downtown Dallas. This experience was well below those other locations, but still very much in the 4.5-star category, as the level of service, decor, and amenities was still far superior to any 4-star hotel. The fitness center is quite extensive, which is a major positive, and the lounge in the lobby for drinks is quite good. Also, they upgraded me for a fee to a residence, so I was able to have a washer-dryer unit and microwave, which was great! That being said, here are the things that made this somewhat less than a 5-star experience for me:

1) I think the main issue is that the hotel is built very long ago, so there are infrastructure issues that make it hard to fully renovate. As such, the room are pretty dated, and they do not have large bath tubs for soaking. I looked at one of the suites on the hotel side, but this bath tub was also not very deep.

2) There is substantial noise from drag-racing and the House of Blues, going on at all hours of the night, even on weekdays. I had to use white noise on my phone and ear plugs to try to sleep, but this is a problem, as the hotel is not adequately sound-proofed, given its age.

3) The spa facility is pretty small with just a few lockers, one steam room, and one shower. No sauna, no indoor whirlpool, no plunge pool. I did get a massage at the spa, which was fantastic! There is a whirlpool outside, which was very strong jets, which also was great. The main issue is the actual spa experience with limited size and lack what was mentioned above.

Overall, as stated, this is 4.5 rating for me, but the external noise is an issue that it is very difficult to solve. The staff was extremely friendly and accommodating, but these are structural issues with the building itself and the location. If I return to Houston, I would likely try a different 5-star property.

Four Seasons Houston is incredible. I've stayed at 17 Four Seasons in the US and a number of them internationally. What I have learned is they are not all equal. Some have incredible service, some are in amazing locations (Lanai being a favorite location) and while none of them have ever been "bad" there are locations that clearly stand out.

What makes Houston stand out is the service. Wow! It's really something special here.

Our trip started with the concierge team going way above and beyond for our trip.

We are here for our daughters cheer competition. I let them know ahead of time that's why we were coming. Steven Kung on the concierge team asked me a number of questions and helped to organize a very memorable arrival.

Upon arrival there was a red carpet with a dozen team members cheering my daughter on with cheer poms, and banners with her name.

They went out of their way to sign the banners and since I requested the room be decorated the most thoughtful decorations I have ever seen.

As an example a large wooden sign that said "Go Ellie" that the team had taken the time to write personal notes of encouragement on.

My daughter was PUMPED upon arriving!

It doesn't stop there. Every request is met with enthusiasm, support and outstanding service.

The room service staff is so friendly and helpful as well.

We are in the Ambassador suite and the room is very nice. Spotless, great views, good layout, and all the amenities you would want.

Every team member I encountered has been great.

This sort of experience doesn't happen randomly. It comes from an entire team that truly cares about their guests. It starts with management setting the stage and doing as they want their team to do leading by example.

Four seasons corporate if you are reading this take note of how this hotel is being run. I became a Four seasons residential owner because I love the brand. I've seen it all both at Four Seasons and your competing brands (Auberge, Aman, Mandarin, etc) and Four Seasons Houston is one of the best examples of what the Four Seasons brand embodies and thrives to be.

Four Seasons Houston is at the top of my list!

We really enjoyed our stay at this hotel! The kids had a great time in the swimming pool — it was perfect since it was pretty hot outside. The rooms were spacious and comfortable. We ordered room service twice (dinner and breakfast), and everything was absolutely delicious. The only thing — the food by the pool was very spicy! I get that it’s Texas and things are supposed to be “hot”, but maybe still not the hot dogs for the kids, right? 😄

Exceptional experience staying here during our road trip. Wonjae made the night extra special with his attention to detail & in making the speakeasy experience possible. Would say he went out of his way to make the stay memorable.

name: Embassy Suites by Hilton Houston Downtown

reviews.text: My family and I recently stayed here for the Houston Chevron Marathon weekend. This is the second time that we stay here, and it is THE BEST choice! Every person was patient and helpful; Claude was awesome and the manager was HIGHLY accommodating.The women in the dining area were friendly and attentive. The rooms were spacious and absolutely perfect for our needs. The location was convenient and accessible despite the various road closures around downtown. We will definitely stay here again!!!!

Barn doors…are we in 1800s..why every hotel room has to have a barn door let alone bathroom of all places?? Thats the most ridiculous thing ever. There is zero privacy and there is a huge gap between the door and the wall. All sounds and smells travel freely in the room…I mean who does that?
The room however was spacious and smelled fresh but in some places it showed its age especially the bathroom looked worn out. Really needs renovations.
The breakfast was included and it was good! Omelette is freshly cooked right in front of you.
Valet parking is $60 but you can park across the street for much less and still be able to go in and out (I paid $30 for 28 hours).
The staircase in the lobby had a decent layer of dust..seems like nobody touched it in a while. Front Desk staff could be a little more welcoming.

Had an awesome experience, everyone was so friendly except the bartender at happy hour, but we didn't let her put a damper on our wonderful stay. There was the sweetest guy named Claude who went above and beyond to ensure our special time with our grandkids was a magical one... explaining all the details about the downtown christmas lights and walking us next door to use the pool amenities! He was just truly awesome and we think he should definitely be recognized for his wonderful customer service!

Stayed for several days while visiting Houston on business. My wife joined me on the trip. She is claustrophobic and doesn’t take elevators. I called several times to determine if the hotel had accessible stairs and couldn’t get a definitive answer. Finally a few days before our trip I spoke with Doug and he assured me that the stairs were accessible via the second floor. Surprisingly, when we arrived at the hotel, Doug checked us in and explained again how to access the stairs. This reassured my wife.

Overall, the hotel was nice and the room was clean. The breakfast and evening cocktails were also a nice treat. The restaurant, Hearsay on the Green was convenient and has excellent food. My only issue was the fitness center. It was smaller than I expected and the water station wasn’t always full.

In addition to Doug, Emily and Destiny politely handled several of my requests.

Not a pleasant experience, extremely uncomfortable beds and pillows. The king bed was way too small, nasty smell in the room and no insulation as we could hear all street noise at floor 7 in our rooms. Staff was ok, nothing special. Valet guys are ok - not helpful at all with luggage. Being a Hilton Honors it wasn’t any different experience. The only good thing was the hearsay restaurant. Great food and service.

Gym was nice and clean.

Great place and old library type architecture. The lift wait time is real and it takes a lot during busy hours or work hrs the entire day time.
Has great restaurant and meals as well as coffee shop in the hotel. Located in heart of the downtown and walkable to all downtown offices and buildings. Rooms are spacious and good to stay

I recently stayed at the Hyatt Regency in downtown Houston, and honestly, my experience was a mixed bag. On one hand, the atrium and lobby are genuinely stunning. The atrium gives off a grand, modern feel, and the lobby renovation is clearly recent and done with taste — it feels very “Hyatt.”

But then you walk into the rooms, and it’s like someone forgot to renovate them. The decor feels very dated, and the whole vibe leans way more Holiday Inn than a Hyatt Regency.￼ The gym is also tucked away in the basement and feels old, like they haven’t invested in it as much as they did in the public spaces.  ￼

And the bathrooms — yikes. They feel totally out of place given the classy lobby. Rather than matching the clean, modern design outside, the bathrooms look like they’re stuck in another decade.

That contrast is what really frustrated me: the lobby is all Hyatt, with high design and style, but the rooms feel like something from a much cheaper, older chain. It feels like two different hotels under one roof, and not in a charming “retro meets modern” way — more like “they half cared.”

On the bright side, the staff are fantastic. Everyone was friendly, professional, and genuinely seemed to care about making your stay as pleasant as possible. Whether I had a question at check-in or needed help during the stay, the team delivered.

Nice location for local attractions, room was clean, and service was fantastic. The 24 hour bagel shop is super convenient, and the bar/restaurant in the lobby is also a nice touch.

I had to cut my trip short, leaving in the middle of the night, due to a family emergency back home and Jermaine at the front desk was kind, supportive, and empathetic. The valet service is reasonably priced and another special shout of goes to Joe B, the night shift valet manager, who truly was a light during a really hard time for me. The car was well taken care of and the service was above and beyond. I would certainly recommend this hotel if you are in the area, and I plan to come back again next time I’m in town.

Came here for my 33rd Birthday as my first trip to the city of Houston and I was a bit disappointed with the price I paid for and the room I had.. it wasn't a bad experience but definitely could have been better.

I usually like to let hotels know via phone and in my booking about my birthday and how I'm celebrating getting a room to have some sort of gift or room gift upon arrival since I've received this from other hotel chains, just to not have anything in my room or any vouchers/discounts for any of the restaurants in the hotel or even

While my request for a quiet room was fulfilled, my request to have a light-fixture that was filled with something like pests/dusts particles was not fulfilled even after personally requesting this to be looked at and cleaned.

What was good was the clean room (other than the light fixture), great ameneties, refillable filtered-water fountains downstairs,  helpful front desk associates and available resutaunts/bar that was in the hotel with a garage that's across the street from the hotel where you can park your car.

Overall, not a bad stay but for the price I payed.. not really worth it. I probably won't be staying here again.

Stayed here for the Houston marathon in January. The walk to George R Brown was only 10 minutes.
Very accommodating, staff held onto our bags during the marathon. We kept the car in their garage parking, they did inform us an additional $50 would be charged to the room if we didn’t remove the car by 4PM.
Rooms were large but Did have road noise as expected.
Only wished their breakfast was available before 7AM so hotel guests can eat before the marathon.

This was my husband and I first time staying at this location. We booked a room to celebrate our anniversary and New year's. Upon arrival, self parking was easily located. The hotel atmosphere was clean and pleasant. The lobby was beautifully decorated Disney-themed Christmas displays which added a special and joyful touch. Check-in however was slow as a Honors member, I expected quicker services and a pleasant greeting by staff which was disheartening.  Our room was comfortable and had a great view to see fire works. However the bathroom was not properly cleaned as there were long strains of hair on the shower wall. Despite that our stay was enjoyable. Shout out to Charedea, who was bright and personalable.  She also assisted us at Check-out and comp'd our parking in honor of our anniversary. Overall, our stay was great and we will be returning.

I've stayed repeatedly in this hotel, and feel very confident in giving it 5 stars with one caveat.

The hotel is large, and it's nice. The rooms have always been clean, and at least all of the rooms that I've stayed in have always been quite large. Views are good. Food is good. Bathrooms have got excellent showers with water pressure that means you can shower in just a minute or two as it's so easy to clean the shampoo out of your hair.

The only negative is check in desk related. This isn't a one time experience - this has been the case on every single visit for the 7 years that I've been coming here, 3-5 visits per year. This hotel has a line for Honors members and another general line for non members. Joining the HH line is always a mistake. The staff never call people up from that line with any sort of priority - quite the opposite. 2 people in that line will definitely take longer than 20 in the other line. I have often observed people joining the general line after somebody had already spent several minutes in the HH line and that newly arrived general line person will get seen while the HH person is continued to be made to wait. With the digital key service in the app it is less often necessary to even go the m to the desk, but if you do have to go to the desk save yourself the frustration of the Honors line.

I stayed at the Hilton Americas Houston and honestly had a wonderful experience from start to finish. The hotel is super clean, modern, and very well-taken care of. The lobby looks beautiful, the rooms are spacious, and the amenities are great — especially the pool and the fitness center. The location is perfect too, right next to the convention center and so many great spots in downtown.

The staff were all friendly and professional, and the service felt smooth everywhere we went. I also want to give a big shoutout to Benny at the valet. He was extremely welcoming, fast, and helpful the moment we arrived. He greeted us with a smile, handled everything quickly, and made the whole check-in experience feel easy. Benny definitely added a personal touch that made our stay even better.

Overall, amazing hotel, great service,

⭐️⭐️⭐️⭐️ Excellent Stay and Outstanding Service!

I had a wonderful experience staying at the Hilton Americas. From the moment I arrived, the staff made me feel welcome and well cared for. The valet, Beni, was incredibly professional, friendly, and efficient—he greeted me with a smile and made parking quick and stress-free. The front desk team was also exceptional, handling check-in smoothly and making sure I had everything I needed for a comfortable stay.

The room was spotless, spacious, and quiet, and the amenities were top-notch. The staff’s attention to detail and genuine hospitality truly made a difference. I highly recommend this hotel to anyone visiting the area—I’ll definitely be returning!

Let me start off by saying I wanted to given your 4.5 stars that turned into 5 star service. I took off a lil based on my room bc I did the early check in and my room still needed a few details attended too. The hotel sent someone immediately to address it and texted me multiple times to make sure I was ok. They offered to change my room but i decided to stay. The housekeeping manage the next day wrote me a sweet apology and left me a sweet treat and a comfy robe which at 1st i was like I don’t need a room but ended loving that I had this cozy item. I checked out the gym bar and restaurant on premises and all staff was kind. I recommend staying here bc everyone was super kind from the guys outside to front desk to housekeeping. Oh and I was so glad to choose this hotel I was here for conference and visited another conference hotel and it felt cold in comparison. This hotel it really paired well with my personality warm, happy and cheerful. The vibe for Christmas was spot on and I was so glad to see their chocolate display. So if looking for a hotel where you feel seen heard safe and happy this is it!

name: Holiday Inn Express & Suites Houston-Dwtn Conv Ctr by IHG

reviews.text:
I can't believe I'm having to write a review about such a negative experience at an IHG hotel. I've been staying with IHG for a long time and this is literally the worst hotel I have ever stayed in. Lots of different things. But what made me walk out at midnight was I was about to go to bed and there was a roach in the bed. I called front desk, he said I could move rooms but said he couldn't meet me at that room with the  keys because he couldn't leave his little post. (I had to pack up all my stuff and I had just got out of the shower. I know plenty who have left the area just for a couple minutes). Well either way, I opened the door to the new room and there was another.. larger roach after I turned the lights on in the bathroom. DONE .. NOT ME.

The phones didn't work the first night. The room phones or the actual phone to the hotel. My TV didn't work. Couldn't get signal at all. And I needed a plate from the breakfast area for my dinner, but since it was moved upstairs, she didn't know how to get into the meeting room and couldn't get me a plate. That is small... But still... INSANE! never stay here if you have a choice. RUN! Stay in your car rather than this place.

⭐️ 1 STAR – A COMPLETE NIGHTMARE FROM CHECK-IN TO CHECK-OUT 😤🐜💳
I checked into Holiday Inn Express Downtown Houston Convention Center and what followed was one of the most chaotic, unprofessional hotel experiences I’ve ever had.
Let’s talk rooms first.
Room 242
The privacy lock DID NOT WORK. I was literally asked if I wanted to stay in the room anyway because “maintenance isn’t here tonight.” Excuse me???
Room 343
Couldn’t even get into the room because of a lock/padlock issue.
Room 243
FULL BLOWN ANT INFESTATION. Not one or two ants. An entire ant circus 🐜🐜🐜. Completely uninhabitable. I have video proof and photos.
Because of all this, I had to cut my stay short, cancel my plans, and drive HOURS back home unexpectedly. No offer to relocate me to another Holiday Inn. No effort to make it right. Nothing.
Now the billing mess.
I booked through Priceline. The room was paid with a third-party card. My personal Cash App card was ONLY for a $50 incidental deposit.
After checkout, the hotel: • Tried to charge my card $104.60 ❌
• Then tried again for $154 ❌
Both declined by my bank
• STILL hasn’t returned my $50 deposit
• And then a staff member named Brandon had the audacity to threaten my refund over the phone
Yes, the call was recorded.
They kept trying to hit my card AFTER all of this, as if I owed them money for a stay I couldn’t even safely complete. That’s not a mistake. That’s reckless.
This wasn’t just dirty.
This was unsafe, unprofessional, and borderline predatory behavior.
I’ve escalated this to corporate and attached photos and video because clearly that’s the only way to be taken seriously.
Save yourself the stress.
There are WAY too many hotels in Houston to deal with this mess.

We stayed the day after Christmas specifically to use the indoor pool with our daughter. I called ahead to confirm the pool and hot tub were available and heated.

Unfortunately, the pool was burning hot — as hot or hotter than a hot tub, making it unsafe for our child to swim, while the hot tub was cold. Because of this, our daughter couldn’t swim and was very upset when we had to leave.

I spoke with the front desk and requested a manager, but none was available. The staff member was polite, but they could not fix the issue or transfer our stay to another Holiday Inn. We were allowed to check out, but a refund could not be issued immediately.

We had to leave and book another hotel. This was very disappointing, and I hope we receive our refund, as the main amenities we confirmed ahead of time were unusable.

Absolutely loved my stay. I did have a couple of things I was unsatisfied with but Ahmed was super duper helpful and went above and beyond to help me during my stay. All the employees are lovely and ready to serve with a smile. The complimentary breakfast had such a wide selection, I was surprised they had my favorite oikos yogurt ! The gym was small but it had everything you needed for a basic work out and no ones ever in there so I loved having the whole gym to myself. You do have to let them know ahead of time that you want to use the jacuzzi or it will be cold and takes 15-25 minutes to heat up. There’s a laundry room which was very useful during my stay. My only gripe is that the TVs are way too small and outdated , I was not able to plug in my console for video games. And the cable selection is very limited. No Comedy Central which is what I mainly watch. All in all, I definitely recommend coming back.

Stayed here Oct 4 - 6 2025.  First off. The building is surrounded by major construction.  On the weekends no noise but on Monday morning 730am it woke us up.

The worst part about our room was the really bad mildew smell.  It actually made my girlfriend nauseous.  She is more sensitive than I am. My throat would just get scratchy amd my eyes would itch.

The door to our room was extremely difficult to open and close. To open, it took a couple should and hip hits ... to close, we would have to pull so hard the door handle was bending.

Inside the room, you can see where a much larger flat screen TV ise to be mounted on the wall but was missing.   Instead we had a 24 inch TV just sitting on top the desk.  It was NOT a smart TV.  Only had 6 local channels to pick from.

Parking lot here is very small.  Maybe 20 cars.  Otherwise it is street parking (and good luck with the construction because many areas are blocked off). There is a pay lot across the street.  Once we got a parking spot in the lot, we left the vehicle and just walked or took a Lyft

I travel quite a bit each year and it takes a lot for me to post reviews. I WILL NOT BE STAYING HERE AGAIN.

Hope this helps 🙏

name: The Sam Houston Hotel, Tapestry Collection by Hilton

reviews.text: I really like this hotel. Service was great, room was really clean, and it has a nice aesthetic. I run hot and the AC kept me cold so thats a huge plus for me. Unfortunately the hot water wasnt working later on during our stay. We were told it had something to do with the storm. The had a plumber rush out to fix and got it up and running. Receptionist and staff were all very polite. Its located in the middle of downtown so once you valet, you really dont need your car anymore, you can walk everywhere.

One of the best hospitalities I have experienced at hotels in the western side of the world. From all the people on different shifts at check in, the restaurant, the valet, to the over the phone communication.

The interiors of the hotel are also one of the unique ones. The building gives rustic charms but their is chic in the decor items to amenities.

It was super kind to provide an extra pack of goodies for Silver guests. Makes you feel valued as a customer.

The only downside is that the television didn't work in my room. Which was a slight bummer, though it isn't a must have for me.

It's a super peaceful spot, great bedding, ample lighting, and really an over all you'll-find-everything-you-need situation! ♡

Still staying at the hotel so will report back with an update and maybe a photo when I haven't upheaveled the sheets/place!

Update: The experience only got better from the amazing reception hosts helping with selecting the best beverage in the house to literally being delivered a microwave to my room for warming up my late night snacks! Loved the cosy street views from the windows and the rainy streets of Houston. Absolutely recommend this place! This time,adding a photo of the lovely interiors! The olive/sage foresty green is such a vibe ♡

Modern hotel with  a very sophisticated vibe.  Love the concept.  Would have left a 5 star review, if it wasn't for the no hot water for more than 12 hours. Other than that, rooms are super comfortable, staff is great and would stay here again.

What a cute hotel! The rooms were well appointed and the bed comfy. The shower was great pressure and hot as heck! I checked in late and smoothly, was offered extra waters, and a noon checkout. Service perfect too. No complaints! Plus it was a 5 min walk to the courthouse and easy for rideshare.

The decor is lovely but once you start looking closely, it’s a bit…worn. Loose fixtures galore (wall sconces, bathroom faucets, etc). Paint slapped on things to make them seem newer. They were obviously renovating while we were there so maybe just an unfortunate in-between time to catch them. Room was tidy but not clean - hair in far corner of shower, blood(?) stain on duvet. Rippling carpet difficult to wheel luggage over. All switches felt very dirty. Okay location. A lot to do in walking distance but traffic and parking will be an obstacle. We used the valet service. It was decent. They did accommodate an early check-in, which was nice, but why have a room ready that isn’t at its cleanest? Not the worst, not the best.

Stayed here for the Houston Marathon weekend. The rooms and everything were maintained really well. For being all booked for a busy weekend they managed it all well.
One note, they need slightly better customer service with check in and check out.
Offered to help with my bags after I already walked in and before I checked in- this could be offered at or after check in.
At check out, it was confusing if I was good to go or if I they needed anything else. A simple, “thanks for staying, have a nice day” would’ve been clear that I was good to go.

I stayed at the JW Marriott Houston downtown on NYE with my girlfriend. Michelle at the front desk greeted me warmly and welcomed us in the room with a welcome card and a free bottle of prosecco. The service is truly above and beyond. We slept a little late towards the morning and the curtains were so dark that we couldn’t tell 😂🥹 The furniture and bathroom are also really modern. Highly recommend booking this property!

The staff was very nice.   I would have liked to have been advised that there was construction going on directly outside the front.  The TV in one of our rooms had white spots,  the desk chair had the leather worn off.   When checking in and checking out, both times the person who was processing me had to ask their coworker to help because they were having 'computer problems'

It's a nice hotel, but I feel there are better options.

From the moment my friend and i walked into the hotel we were treated with amazing service. The staff is wonderful and so accommodating! Michelle at the front desk was so helpful and went out of her way to rececommend places for us to visit and eat. Everything she recommended was wonderful! I would highly recommend staying at this Marriott! Can't wait to come back!

Overall was disappointed with the room. Booked the king suite and was very underwhelmed. We booked with The Hotel Collection and received no upgrade.

The "living room" was tiny and only had a mini fridge, a desk, a couch and a mounted TV on the wall. The couch didnt even face the TV.

The TV in the bedroom and living area both had burn-ins on the screen which was very annoying.

Did not feel like a true 5-star hotel. Wish we would have booked somewhere else and overall regret staying here.

Really liked this little place. If you are looking for a corporate perfect and shiny hotel, this isn't necessarily it. But if you are looking for simple, comfortable, with a great staff, this is a great fit. There is a small kitchenette - just be aware that you may need to bring or buy items to fully leverage it. But having a full fridge and a microwave for leftovers was fantastic. Lots of good food options in walking distance. Overall, a cozy stay with great hands-on service from the staff.

Service and Room were excellent. Very nice and clean. The only downside is where it is at, for instance no parking around, but to get a parking voucher you have to park 2 blocks away in a parking lot. Overall, it was not bad at all just having to park blocks away was the downfall.

Went to a concert at the Toyota Center and this could not have been a better place to stay! Not even a full block walk away, it took max 2 minutes walking.

The room was cozy and clean, and there was a kitchenette which was so convenient! The staff was so helpful too! Victor at the front desk waited for us to check in late when our flight was delayed and was continuously kind and helpful throughout our stay.

I will absolutely stay here again when coming to Houston in the future!
We really enjoyed the stay, great location and friendly staff. The staff really cares about you and treats you like family. The rooms are clean and comfortable. 5 stars. John is a wonderful owner!

I chose this hotel because the location is good for Houston Marathon. The owners are very friendly and so kind. I was able to extend my stay until 1pm free of charge. So, I could take a shower after marathon and I checked out.

Excellent place for special occasions. Be prepared to spend $100 a person if having drinks, a shared app, main, and dessert.

The focaccia bread was really good, particularly the dips. I had the scallops...5 large and tender. There were no sides, and I wish I had realized that or I might have ordered one. That however left room for dessert.

The coffee Gelato was perfect. The chocolate cake was unremarkable, and the custard wasn't my cup of tea (the 3 of us shared).

They make a great old fashioned but use small pieces of ice which was disappointing. These should have one big cube or it stars getting watered down.

Great atmosphere right on the green. The service was friendly and professional.

Atmosphere was casual elegant perfect if you want something upscale without feeling overly formal. The setting was beautiful, especially with the park views, and it definitely feels like a great spot for a special occasion.
The service was good, not great, but still pleasant and professional. Nothing stood out as exceptional, yet nothing took away from the evening either. Food was well prepared and enjoyable, and the ambiance really carried the experience. Overall good experience.

New Year’s Eve dinner, filet mignon with potatoes and broccolini was the best filet I’ve ever had. The sauce that came with was perfect!  Service was excellent!  Cocktails unique and delicious! Thank you for a lovely experience!! ⭐️⭐️⭐️⭐️⭐️
We had a great waiter that gave us great recommendations. I was going to get something else but he persuaded me and I’m glad I went with his pick. It was a refreshing meal.

In Houston for just a couple of days and decided to stop in for a meal and thankful I did because it was by far the most delicious meal I’ve had in ages. The atmosphere is relaxed Bohemian vibes with a level of sophistication and the staff was awesome.

I had an amazing time at the Downtown Aquarium. Never have I been to an aquarium that has a restaurant, stingray exhibit, rides, shark tunnels, and a tiger!! It was absolutely beautiful and I had the best experience. I totally would go back just to redo everything. The only downside is that the aquarium is on the smaller side, however I think everything else makes it worth the price!! Can’t wait to come back.

A Magical Birthday Under the Sea! 🐠✨
We had a fantastic time celebrating my daughter’s birthday at the Houston Downtown Aquarium! If you’re looking for a venue that combines a great meal with a built-in "wow" factor, this is it.
The Highlights:
* Atmosphere: The dining room is stunning. Sitting right next to the massive floor-to-ceiling tanks was mesmerizing—watching the sharks and rays glide by while we ate made the celebration feel truly special.
* The Food: Everyone at the table was impressed! The seafood was fresh and the service was professional.
* Value: While it’s definitely a "tourist attraction" price point, it felt completely worth it for the experience and the convenience of having the exhibits right there.
It was the perfect spot for a memorable family birthday. We walked away with great photos and even better memories!

Worst Aquarium I’ve ever been to, but still giving it a 4/5 because its not just an aquarium. This place needs a better name or to be represented as something other than an aquarium - while they have aquatic life on display, its very limited. What they have in addition to the fish, makes up for what it lacks. Fair-style rides, shark trains, concessions, city views, a restaurant with aquatic views AND EVEN a TIGER?! it makes it a great experience for all - even those who wouldn’t be excited about an aquarium.

This is a great aquarium with an educational train ride that the kids will love. It takes you through an aquarium tunnel and outside where they have an entertaining shark bit (missing “shark” pops out of the water). Eating at the restaurant was fun. You get to see all the fish swimming in the aquarium around you. They have a little theme park outside with some kiddie rides, splash pad etc. We loved coming here

Very disappinting... I was expecting a fun ( preety big) aquarium/amusement park. This park could be fun- if you are 3 years old. This thing is ridiculous. I wasted so much moeny on tickets for this thing. The aquarium itself is very tiny and it only took me 15 minutes to go around. The tanks were like going to an aquarium to see guppies. Again. And again. And again....  the same grey small classic riverfish in almost every enclosure. The acual interesting part were the tigers. But I was to discusted by thier environment to accualy enjoy it. These 4 poor beutiful creatures are living in an indoor plastic small decorated enclosure suffering with constant annoying temple music around them. Not surprizing to jear that they only feed the sharks 3 times a week as well. This is animal misery. There wasnt even anyone else there. Even the gift shop was small and super expensive. The rides were all either baby rides, or closed, and even the open rides looked like they were under construction, and the staff were hanging with friends using thier phones instead of monitoring the rides. The place was so empty - I was the only person there, and it was kind of creepy. I thought that the whole aquarium was 3 stories tall, but no. The whole 2nd and 3rd floor is for thier restaurant ( completly empty) and thier bar and ballroom ( probobly empty too) Dont ask why you need a ballroom for an aquarium... I thought that the shark train ride would be cool, but the train is a little kids size, and you only spend 2 minutes with real sharks. After that, you just spend the rest of the time looking at a  beutiful ( not really) plastic shark and you pass through a not so wonderful parking lot...

The biggest thing holding this AMC back is the lack of free parking. I scoured Google and Yelp reviews alike discussing this, went 20 minutes early, and still got misinformation and failed to get free parking. The only saving grace is that they release their movie schedule early, so I was able to snag two free tickets using a Fandango discount before it expired.

As of October 2025: If you pay to enter a parking garage, you won't get parking validated. So basically..good luck finding a garage that doesn't make you pay on entry on a weekend.. We parked at Garage 5 which is pay-on-entry on weekends for $18, and they do apply that to drinks or concessions so I topped up $2 for a large margarita. Don't get a frozen margarita - it's not truly frozen all the way through, more like slushy on top with a tequila shooter on the bottom that you have to mix up (2/5 for the drink, at least it was a bit strong).

We only had three other people in our theater and one couple left midway through. The seats don't fully recline out and were a tad worn, and the entire theater itself gives a dated old-timey vibe because of how empty the establishment felt. The movie and lights worked fine.

Overall, I wouldn't come back unless it was a weekday to have my parking validated.

Really wish AMC would get this location together, as it's the only theater in Houston's downtown! But I haven't seen anyone in the reviews mention my biggest concern, which is that the exit sign lights do not dim adequately during the screenings, causing a green cast on screen. This has happened on at least two different screens at this theater (I'm posting a picture I took of the issue when I saw Conclave like a year ago, but I've had the same issue since). It's very distracting, and so I will drive the extra 15 minutes to another location to avoid it.

To management - please fix that one issue, and I promise I'll come back!! The parking and smell issues other commenters have mentioned may be hard to fix, but I imagine this issue is a relatively simple thing to resolve. Would love to edit this review if it's fixed.

You have to pay for parking, the workers don’t tell you where the vale parking is, then the seats were old and hard and uncomfortable, the people behind us kept kicking our seats, and the theater was hot even after my husband asked them to turn on the a/c, for the price we paid for tickets and food was ridiculous, 7.00 for a bottle water, the bathrooms are not clean at all, they allow homeless people watch movies and stinks, for a down town theater this place was horrible. Do not recommend at all. Will definitely not be coming back.

I love this theater. I don't understand the negative reviews based on a few tears in seats. As you can see from my picture most seats are great, wide and comfy and with a large drink holder arm rest. It's a great theater been coming for years and will come back always. The staff is always professional the food is always good and the movies are the latest. Park underground and you can walk right up, the parking is free if you go here.

The customer service is absolutely awful and the theaters are nasty with horrible cleanliness. Do not play the arcade games (claw machines) as they are by Kiddleton and the company can’t do anything if something goes wrong. We spent $20 on the machines for our kids only for the machines not to work. We asked the staff but they refused to do anything. Don’t even bother going to Kiddleton as they won’t do anything either.

If you’re traveling on government business, avoid this hotel.

Although this hotel advertises government-rate rooms, they choose to serve only the highest-tier federal employees—not contractors—even though we follow the same travel rules. I was denied check-in just as severe weather was approaching. Fortunately, I found a nearby hotel that also honors the government rate (without an arbitrary policy about whom they will serve). I can’t imagine what would have happened if my flight had been delayed and this had occurred late at night. Don’t risk it.

A very late review but better than never! I stayed at this hotel from May 28th - June 1st for Dreamcon that was hosted at the George R. Brown Convention Center. I have never stayed at a Westin hotel so I didn't know what to expect but this hotel is one of the nicest hotels I have ever stayed at, maybe even the nicest. The front of house staffing and front guest were very welcoming when we entered and exited. Those who worked in housekeeping, the marketplace, and at the restaurant were all kind and helpful. I wish I knew the name of every staff member that worked during that time but I can at least name two who made our stay amazing, Jestine and DJ. For the hospitality that they gave us, I hope they receive some type of raise or bonus. From big laughters, having great conversations, and being able to help us when we needed something, those two were amazing. This convention is scheduled again for next year and I have already decided that I will be staying here at this hotel once again. I look forward to my stay next year once scheduled! Thank you to everyone who worked those dates during our stay. :)

The Westin Downtown hosted our wedding block and they were absolutely wonderful. The service was top notch, the rooms were clean, modern, and affordable, and the lounge and bar downstairs was the perfect place to have a drink and visit with friends and family. We could not be any happier with our experience!

Decent hotel, with reasonably nice rooms and bathrooms.  However, didn't get any sleep as there were constant pops and cracks coming from the walls all night long, loud enough that we could hear them over our white noise app at full volume.  Outside of toilet bowl had dried feces on it.  Fan in bedroom unusable given how loud it was wghen turned on.  Overall, disappointing Marriott property for us (perennial Titanium member).

I have never been treated so low and unappreciated as a customer. I purchased 2 traditional rooms and 1 parlor room for 2 nights,  a value over $2000.  The management team and staff was unconcerned with my complaints and show us no appreciation as a valued customer.   I think McDonald's show their customers more appreciation.   This was at the Westin downtown houston.  We had a problem with one of the traditional rooms, the pipes had ruptured and water was pouring from the walls in the bathroom.   This ruined our trip because our property was damaged including a designer purse at a value over $800.  The hotel staff didn't not want to fix the problem but throw towels on the floor.  They did not want to compensate us in any way.  They stated the leak was coming from the shower from the running water.  This was a lie.  After our final bill, they charged a unauthorized fee of $200 more on my credit card, because I gave them a bad review online. This people are scammers, stay the hell away.

name: Lucky Strike Houston
address: 925 Bunker Hill Rd, Houston, TX 77024, USA

reviews.text: This place is reasonably priced.  They have packages for up to six people for two hours, a pizza and a pitcher of soda ( with free refills) for around $77.00. Food is not that good but, you can't beat that price.  They can get pretty full and then it is hard to find bowling balls in the smaller sizes and shoes in the smaller sizes. My family and I have been here twice now and I  will go back again.  Where they lack is made up for in their price.

Honestly it's always a fun experience when you have the right people with you. I went one night and was not greeted at all by a server, had to go out the way to finally get someone to help. Isn't normally like that tho. I do miss the heavier balls.

Had a good time for my bettors birthday party.  It was very busy.  My waitress was awesome. Loved that I was able to open a tab. Was not ready for $40/ per person,  but it was unlimited bowling so not bad.

This is my 4 time coming in 6 weeks and it’s amazing as always. I had my first  discrepancy with Groupon but Kevin and Sandra helped rectify the situation QUICKLY. Will definitely be back soon. The music and the drinks were great!

This is a fun place with friendly staff. The interior is clean, with cute booth areas for the lanes, a bar, a private event room and some arcade games.

Our party of four reserved our lane for two hours, and we were able to eat and bowl three games in that time.

They played energetic music with great beats. We danced a little between our bowling turns, which was a ton of unexpected, extra fun.

All in all, we had a really nice time. I look forward to returning at some point.

name: Main Event Stafford
address: 12626 Fountain Lake Cir, Stafford, TX 77477, USA

reviews.text: Main Event was a total vibe! We enjoyed it thoroughly—kids and adults alike had an absolute blast. From pool tables, Lazer tag and bowling to arcade games and the high ropes course, there was nonstop fun for everyone. Such a great place for family time and making memories. Highly recommend! 🎳🎮🙌

Location is very clean and staff is friendly. We enjoyed playing bowling and the arcade. I'm a little upset with my appetizer, pickles came clumped together, but everything else was good. My children enjoyed themselves and I found plenty of things for adults to do as well.

We had such a great time at Main Event, and a huge part of that was thanks to our waitress, Kae! From the moment we were seated, she was friendly, attentive, and incredibly professional. She made sure our drinks stayed full, our food came out just right, and that we had everything we needed without ever feeling rushed.

Kae’s positive attitude, patience, and genuine care for our experience really stood out and made our visit even more enjoyable. Customer service like hers makes all the difference, and she truly went above and beyond.

Main Event was fun, lively, and well run, but Kae absolutely took the experience to the next level. We’ll definitely be back and hope to have her as our waitress again! 👏🏽✨

It was a Great nite all in all,. tha staff was some what friendly,.
They know who there are👏👏👏
Thank to tha ones that made sure we had a good nite. Here's one of tha people who was kind,tho out my lil'z experience. theres two others that I didn't get a chance to get a picture of.
All in all those 3 people 👌

We had a blast during our visit! The bowling experience stood out—smooth lanes, upbeat music, and a fun atmosphere that made the game even better. We also enjoyed laser tag, arcade games, and billiards—so much to do under one roof. The pizza was just okay, but everything else made up for it. It’s the kind of place that brings out your inner kid and is perfect for a casual hangout with friends or family

Paid for my sons birthday party, booked in the crystal room. Party area was not ready for a half hour after the time we booked. Several of the fun attractions are not working as well which was a bummer. The manager, not sure his name, was more than helpful in getting us accommodated and expediting our wristbands and game cards.

It looks a bit run down but we had a great time. Communication seems like it could be better, along with some aesthetic touch ups. There’s an obvious abundance of maintenance work that could be implemented to brighten the place up.

Pizza for the party was good, our hostess was pleasant and helpful as well. We had a great time all in all.

I took my daughters to this place this past Sunday and it really wasn’t worth it. The outside is super dirty and building can’t possibly be up to code. You can tell it was a nice place when it first opened but the current owners don’t care. In the arcade only a few games worked, the skating ring was the best part. They have rides that you can get on but they were outdated and there were no attendants. The place feels like a big ball of germs, touching anything makes you feel dirty. The bowling alley was nice but not it’s in a corner and is easily missed. I didn’t trust the food because the ordering counter was dirty, machines haven’t been cleaned or whipped down. It was a no for me. The staff want friendly, very nonchalant, poor hygiene/super musty and had attitudes. I will say except for the ticket lady at the front and the young men that ran the skating ring. I drive from Katy thinking to try a new place but that drive wasn’t worth it. Save your money because it is expensive and you can’t comfortably enjoy anything!!

We had a blast going to this place. The kids loved it, very affordable, we got the 20$ wristbands that allow all but 3 attractions. Everything is indoors, which was perfect considering that it was 100 plus degrees outside. There was a lot to do there, bowling , skating, arcade games, play areas, Farris wheel, trampoline jump, bumper cars, and more. The lines were very small while we were there. What a great place for all ages. Has a bar in it for adults, they have food  there. We will definitely be visiting again.

I've been regularly coming to the Funplex since the 2000s when I was a child. This place has resonated with me deeply. It has a unique and weird architecture (weird in the best way), with an unmatched ambience and fun activities in each corner. Quite frankly, just walking around is a nice time in itself. I look forward to the Funplex reopening - thank you for so many memories, big liminal playground! I took these pictures with an old camera, but they're from 2024.

If you read my reviews you would know that I am usually positive and tend to try to always be so. But our experience at the funplex was such a disappointment. I truly hope the owners take a good look at these reviews and try to make some changes. The place was overpriced and half of the games didn’t work. The kids went from game to game not able to play any games they wanted. A ticket to fiesta Texas is cheaper and 100x a better value. The 20 signs at the counter that said “no refunds” was the first red flag. The place is run down and the workers are mostly rude and obviously untrained. This place could be something with better management

We loved it. It was a great experience. Staff is very friendly and helpful. Restrooms clean, great food, great prices and we had a great time playing.
Family friendly.

Came here on a Saturday evening for a birthday party. We had four lanes booked out and I'd recommend a maximum of six players per lane, although they do support more than that.

Rental shoes are quite stretched out here. I actually sized down nearly 2 full sizes (usually wear a 7.5, ended up using 6's) to fit snugly.

The bowling balls are set up from ~8lbs to ~15lbs in order. I couldn't really tell, but it seemed like the smaller balls also tended to have smaller finger holes on average.

There are some "game" options to bowl with that ultimately boil down to 1 bowl x 5 frames, 2 bowls x 5 frames, and 2 bowls x 10 frames. Kids may enjoy the Angry Birds game, which is 2 bowls x 5 frames but if you hit all of the birds, you'll effectively get a "strike" (at least, I think that's how it works). For serious bowlers, I'd suggest the "Pro Stats" setup which will give you mph, % of strikes, and things like that. You can put in your e-mail to get your scorecard or even set up using an app, which are great modern options. The people who weren't as good bowlers ended up trying to go for high speed bowls toward the end of the night (25.7mph was the winner if you're curious).

We had a few incidents where someone bowled during a reset, usually because we were playing a minigame where we expected two rolls but actually ended up being counted as a strike due to whatever game gimmick was going on. During those times, it took the staff about 5-10 minutes to respond and clear the lane.

Lane 20 was also really wrong in the sensors. We called it the charity lane because if you guttered a ball, it would give you between 3-6 points randomly. So, not all of the lanes are perfect.

There's also a little arcade here, and some coin operated basketball games.

Cool that you can order bar food and beer from the attached restaurant.

Overall, a generally positive experience with one lane out of four that was a bit glitchy.

As far as bowling alleys go this one is just great! Clean, good food (the hot dog bun was like really good 😂) , access to kids summer bowling program. Helpful staff! The only reason I’m giving it 4 stars is because the bathroom was out of soap in one side and had a broken dispenser on the other side.

100% recommend! This bowling alley is fantastic. The staff are super friendly. The prices are crazy good, and the atmosphere is nostalgic, yet the technology is updated. Altgough I live 45 minutes away, I can still guarantee I'll be back.

And, try the fries and fried pickles! 😋

Great place- clean and well run….classic bowling alley and a lot less expensive than Pinstripes or Lucky Strike.  The pizza dough is made in house and was really good. We will definitely be back. (SAT 1 PM- busy but no wait)

⭐️⭐️⭐️⭐️⭐️ If I could give 100 stars, I absolutely would! We just celebrated my daughter’s 14th birthday at Main Event Shenandoah, and I am still blown away by the experience.

From start to finish, everything was beyond perfect. I have to start by giving huge thanks to Jordan from the sales team — communication with him was incredibly smooth! Every question I had was answered quickly and thoroughly. I even needed to change our event date and extend our bowling time, and he took care of it right away without any hassle. Truly amazing customer service!

Now, let’s talk about our host, Chris — he was PHENOMENAL. I usually keep my expectations low for birthday parties because there’s always something that doesn’t go as planned, but not this time. Chris made our family feel like absolute VIPs! He went above and beyond in every possible way — making sure food was served hot and on time, helping with all the bowling shoes, and just making sure everyone was having fun.

The birthday package itself was such a great value — we got delicious food (sliders, chicken nuggets, churros, French fries, and unlimited fountain drinks!), 1 hour of bowling across 2 lanes, 2 hours of arcade play, laser tag, and billiards. It honestly felt like a premium experience for such a reasonable price.

The highlight was when Chris offered to let my daughter try the human crane, even though it wasn’t part of the package — she was absolutely thrilled! That small gesture made her day even more special, and it meant so much to us.

This was our very first time celebrating at Main Event Shenandoah, and it will definitely not be the last. The service, the kindness, and the attention to detail were simply outstanding. I’ll be recommending this place to all my friends and family — thank you, Jordan, Chris, and the entire team for making my daughter’s birthday so unforgettable! 🎉💖
