We celebrated part of our Veterans Day here. The games were so fun, well kept, and clean!!!!! Cassandra at the bar was the highlight of our day! She was professional, kind, fun, and knowledgeable towards us and everyone else that we saw her interact with. The food was good also.

This is based off of two birthday parties we've had at this location, both were on a Saturday at 10:30 for the brunch with 10 children end of August/beginning of September.
Last year's party: communication for booking was amazing
Day of the party we had two gentlemen as our party host, one was in training.
Food: wonderful, top your own pancake was a hit. We requested juice and milk for the kiddos and coffee for adults.
Entertainment: they set up monster bowling which the kiddos loved. They had pins set out to decorate, so the kiddos had something to do while guests arrived and waited to eat. 10 dollar playing cards were sufficient for most, but some needed to add kor funds.
There was one other party at the same time, but it didn't interfere with our space. We were able to use the extra table for the adults to sit. The hosts were thoughtful and anticipated our needs. Provided cake cutting, helped moved our items for the transition, offered to get us bowling shoes. They helped us carry out supplies. They were always one step ahead. The only downside is they weren't able to print off the poster bowling like advertised.

This years party:
Again, communication for booking was amazing.
Food was good and the pancakes still a hit. I requested juice and milk, but our host said milk wasn't an option. He checked twice and said they didn't have it. Provided one pitcher of apple juice for 10 kids. 8 kids were able to have half of a cup of juice to drink and a refill was not offered.
Entertainment: the host put a pile of the pins to be decorated on a table where we bowl and walked away. We couldn't find him for shoes, so parents were lining up at the couner to get shoes, so we had to skip decorating pins, so we had time to bowl. He finally arrived after we requested him from another staff, and he provided 3 pairs of shoes. He didn't set up monster bowling and had regular bowling. We had to find a manger to set up the kids bowling and one lane ended up only having bumpers. We had to cut bowling short, so they had time to use the arcade cards. I had to go to the front desk to request him again because we never received our arcade cards. He never provided us with the VIP pass scavenger hunt for our son, but made sure to bring a bill. It was frustrating that this time wasn't as smooth since I had our 4 month old baby with us this time. I should've requested a more experienced host.
The managers went above and beyond to try to make the rest of the party better.  They were very sweet, and the managers have been excellent both times we've visited.
Recommendation: I recommend the brunch bowling, it's a hit with 6-8 year olds. Request an experienced host and know they stick to a strict time table. There is zero room for adults and the only seating is for children. There isn't a place to set gifts or desserts or your supplies or sit down. I brought a box each time and put everything in it and under the pancake table. There is zero space for anything on the food table except what they provide. Request an extra serving table, but know that it infringes on the other parties space.
If something does go wrong request a manager because all the managers have been amazing. Do not have Bryce as your host unless you have a small party and prefer to do things yourself. He needs more training before hosting on his own again.

Came here for my daughter's birthday and she had a good time. Large place lots of games.Staff is all around great. Had the pleasure of having "ASIA" take care of me. She was friendly,  professional,  and took good care of us. If you go,  see her.
This is a great place for birthday parties, or just getting out for the weekend for fun with family friends.

We recently held my son’s birthday party at AMF Winderfern Lanes and everyone had a great time! I loved that they decorated the space before we came and made us feel very welcome and cared for. Our host Zach was just wonderful! He was informative, knowledgeable, attentive, patient, and kind. Thank you AMF and Zach for making my son’s party extra special.

Dirty place! Our hands were black when we were done. I was looking forward to an old school experience, with a good juicy burger. The picture on their website shows a well dressed burger, which was a deciding factor to visit. When you get to the location is when you're told NO lettuce, NO tomatoes, NO onions...why is it called a hamburger and not a sandwich?! Why does your website represent a hamburger? It's very old school 80ish place with badly needed upgrades. The specials are ok but really can't compete with the major players, even on the price. The staff is very knowledgeable and friendly, so no complaints there. The music was also good but it was a very underwhelming experience. I would go back to practice but wouldn't invite company. The liquor should be a little cheaper considering the drab environment of the bar.

This my second time coming! It’s a good chill vibe with family! My kids and I had a great time! Food is good! Customer service is Great! Zachery helped us and was very nice! Of course we will be back! Oh and they have coupons on Groupon that’s a great deal!!

First time coming here with my family and we had a great time. This wonderful woman who works there made our experience amazing. She was very understanding and extremely helpful with a situation we were having she also checked up on us and made sure everyone was having fun. And she was the best! We will be definitely coming back.
The staff was so nice and attentive with the family. I don't think I had been to an alley with this good of a customer service.

First time here and came in to play some arcade games which were so fun and then we got checked in for our reservation and Staff was excellent and the food was amazing. Such a great place for date night!  Ask for Lala when you come in, she was AMAZING and she will make sure you have everything you’ll need

I haven't been here since they revamped the place and changed the name. I kind of miss the old place. It's true, this place is more updated and modern. they have all you can bowl on Friday and Saturday night from 9 to 1 AM for like $23 plus you have to buy your shoes. I bought one alcoholic drink that was damn near $20 after the tip, so the bowling is a bargain

The lane was not perfect. It would give us strikes when we had two pins standing up. There's not enough balls for everybody. You have to go grab them off of their lanes if you don't have what you need.

They don't enforce the rules very well here. The lady next to me was bowling in sandals. They also let kids run out everywhere in the bowling area.

They play music, but you can hardly hear it when you're bowling. It's like the levels are off and I need to turn the bass down. it's a bowling alley, not a Cadillac.

The thing I hated the most was, they took the big seats away that you sit in that are at your lane. They put these big tables in its place, but not every lane has a table near the bowling lane. Some are up in the walkway.

also, if you use the lane service to order a drink. It only goes up to number 20 in the app. So if you're in lane #27, you can't order from lane service. It's kind of stupid.

Had a great time! Lala took exceptionally good care of us throughout our time there. Arcade had some fun stuff and the lanes were exactly what you want. Ample well lit parking. Pricing pretty decent (coupon codes easily available on Google).  Will be back!

We had a blast! 1st time at this location. Booked reservation online,  was super convenient! Everyone was so kind and professional from check-in till the end.  Our Waitress was a gem! We will definitely return!

awesome establishment, they have really good food. My favorite is the garlic parmesan fries and the steak quesadillas, a game room for the kids bar for the adults if yall are looking for a spot to take the kids or have a fun outing with friends this is the spot. they also have a bowling league ask for info at the front desk about the bowling league. there are plenty great drink selections and appetizers if you love wings the have boneless and classic wings. come check em out this summer or on the weekend when you have free time. this place is also wheelchair accessible. and bumper bowling on request.

Brought my 3 year old to bowl. Staff set up bumpers and got the “ball roller” set up for him. Smaller shoes are a toddler 9 so be aware any smaller, they will wear their own shoes.

Staff was very kind and patient as we asked questions and got set up.

Bathrooms were clean. Only thing, I didn’t see an infant changing station but my 6 week old slept the entire time so didn’t end up needing it.

We will be back. Thanks for the hospitality.

⭐️⭐️⭐️⭐️⭐️
Super fun place! The environment is really clean and welcoming. It’s not too expensive either, $50 for an hour for up to 6 people, with shoes and everything included in the price. They also have ice cream, a small restaurant, and even lockers to store your things. It was really fun and easy to learn how to play, even if you’re a beginner. Definitely recommend if you’re looking for a good time with friends or family!

We Always have the best time when we come to emerald bowling 🎳 the food is always good the assistance is great there and I took my son here before he goes back to college..and per usual he beat mom n stepdad
They treat us so kind when we come there to have our Special Olympics students bowl.

Bowling here was amazing!! The location area was a little more on the dangerous side but there was extremely bright lights around the entire parking lot. Staff members were nice. I had both option of paying per game or by the hour. It was fairly clean and had a vintage look to it!

name: AMF Diamond Lanes
address: 267 N Forest Blvd, Houston, TX 77090, USA

reviews.text: AMF Diamond Lanes' staff are very user friendly. The lanes are maintained. Sometimes, as with most bowling centers, there are approach issues. Staff immediately rectifies any problems. Ample parking. Reasonable pricing for food, beverages and bowling. I'm there at least once a week.

Mr Phil helped me with improving my game seeing how I’m brand new. Turns out he’s the manager. David treated me at the front and gave awesome pointers aswell. It’s a crazy awesome vibe and everyone makes it feel like a family out here. There’s everyone from pros to brand new players like myself.
Thank you to the server Skye for being extremely patient with me while I made my order. The food is perfect out there. I’m a new regular!

Our family enjoyed our time there. The inside is pretty nice and up to date, may not look like it on the outside! We bought a groupon deal was worth it! We will do again! THE BUFFALO WINGS ARE DELICIOUS! The family really loved them! The pizza was really good too...
Had a blast with the family. Food was good, the manager was really attentive when needed.  Definitely the new spot for my family and friends to bowl.
I am grateful for the kind staff, made the experience so much more enjoyable. My friends and I will be coming back!!! I had so much fun and the environment was genuinely fun

I love this place.  I work nearby and had previously also worked nearby so I have been coming here for about 10 years.  It is kinda an older clean place.  I have had two birthday parties here and some great times.  Now I am not a great bowler, or even that good to be honest ... especially with my limited attention span but this place is clean, with ok food and great drinks!  Yes my good man Trent has got you on all types of beverages.  So sure there are more fancier and hip places to bowl but for hassle free fun, this is my place, palace lanes

Surprised how many negative reviews there are here.  This place is charming in an old-school way.  I've gone there bowling with adults and with groups of kids (boy scouts, birthday parties, and just on a whim) and always had a good time.  I'm not sure what people expect but it's a local landmark that most people appreciate.

As for questions about reserving.. Since they have no website, I'll share what I know about kids parties.  Last week I went to try to reserve a birthday party.  Unfortunately the week I wanted was taken up by a weekend tournament.  The fellow behind the counter, who was courteous and helpful,did his best to try to accommodate me to no avail. (That's not his fault but just a timing issue combined with trying to reserve one week before the date.)

What he did tell me, while showing me the form, is that they do reserve there and that there is a small deposit for each lane (I can't recall exactly so I won't say but it was cheap), they recommend that you reserve 3 -4 weeks in advance, and that it was $11 per child for an hour of bowling.  They calculate 4 kids per lane in their reservation scheme so in my case (had I tried to make a reservation earlier) a party for 12 kids that would have been three lanes for an hour $132 bucks which is not bad at all.

Very friendly folks.    It's old school to be sure and if small details like a carpet that looks to have been there for ages or bowling balls that speak of years of enjoyment bother you, then there are surely more expensive, modern, and more corporate alleys out there but for the price, location, and a quirky charm this alley is wonderful.

By the way - there were two kids parties already happening when I went to try to reserve my party at either end of the alley with all the kids enjoying themselves.

My family and I went there last night and it was terrible.  The place is old, smells like smoke. The balls are old, the machines are old and kept scoring incorrectly but since we were there to just have fun we didn't ask to move.  We had already moved from the first lane because it wasn't working.  Then the people in the lane next to us started smoking.  Yes, smoking is allowed.  I was shocked but I asked about it and the lady said that because they are not in the Houston City limits they don't have to follow those laws.  They are in South Side place.  Anyway, we won't be going back here, which is unfortunate because we live in the area.  We'll have to drive out to the suburbs for the newer better bowling experience.  We went to one in Webster that's awesome but it's new and doesn't smell dirty and smoky.  We are gonna try the one on Bunker Hill next.

I've been here many times before and always had a good experience.  This time, not so much.  The difference was all previous times, it was just me and my family.  Just showed up and everything was wonderful.

This time since I'd always had a positive experience here, I thought it would be a good place to get my softball team together for a team building event.  I scheduled a time for the team to show up.

I called Palace Bowling Lanes about 2 weeks prior to our scheduled outing to confirm that we'd be able to get a couple of lanes.  I was told there were no leagues or other things that would cause us issues for the date I had chosen.   I asked if I could reserve a couple of lanes and was told that they were first come first served. So no, I could not reserve any lanes.

So, on the date that we were to meet up, I showed up AN HOUR prior to the time we had scheduled to make sure that we could get the lanes we needed.

When I went in I went up to the desk and told the guy that I'd called earlier and had a group coming in at 1pm and asked if they had lanes.  Only about half of the lanes were in use.  He said that they had plenty of lanes.

I found a seat and waited the hour.  My team started to arrive and I went back up to the desk to go ahead and get the lanes.  At this time another guy was working the desk and he tells me that we can only have the lanes for an hour.  I ask why since this is the first I'd heard that our time was going to be limited and there were still half of the lanes empty.

He tells me that there are several birthday parties and a cub scout group coming in and they are going to need the rest of the lanes.

So it appears that if you are going to reserve many lanes they WILL RESERVE lanes for you but if you are just going to ask for a couple, you're on your own.

I'm still freaken mad about this and will never schedule anything else at this place. I'm not one to write up bad reviews, I believe this is my first.  But everything about this experience just infuriates me.  Heck, even if the first guy had said, oh by the way, you're only going to be able to bowl for an hour, I could have informed everyone in my group that was going to show up and we could have just met somewhere else.

I first went her on field trip with my middles school and then I even celebrated my 13th birthday. I actually learned to bowl here. The shoes they have to rent are a bit worn, so bring your own if you're pick about those things. I would give it 5 stars but the last time I went was with my family on Father's Day, and I remember smelling cigarette smoke and the area where the food was served was closed.

name: Copperfield Bowl
address: 15615 Glen Chase Dr, Houston, TX 77095, USA

reviews.text:
This is the best bowling alley I have been so far. It’s a shame it’s so far away from me but I keep coming back because of how beautiful the lanes are here. It’s always well oiled, clean and maintained.

The alley has a great open space so u can bowl freely without bumping into over lanes next to you

The pins are very response and doesn’t get stuck like other unmaintained bowling alleys.

100% recommend if trying to bowl professionally or even casually.
Nice bowling alley. Friendly staff. Lanes are in good shape. The pro shop is really knowledgeable. Wish they had unlimited bowling though.

Copper-field bowl is a great place to bowl. I like how they re modeled the place last year and it is nicer than before. The seats are nicer, the screens for bowling are nicer, everything is just so much better which I really like. I hope they continue to keep the bowling alley in tip top shape at all times. I bowl there quite often and I like how you can pay by the hour if you do not want to pay per game.
My family and I had such an amazing time. The staff is very friendly and made us feel very comfortable. This was also the cleanest bowling alley I have ever been to.

First time here and first time to bowl in a while.  Staff were welcoming, friendly, and helpful.  Did the hourly lane rental (we did 2 games for 2 for an hour); seems they don't do per-game pricing on the weekend.  Had a lot of fun.  Scoreboard screens have cute animations for different plays.  There is a small video arcade and a snack bar.  Would love to come back again.

The service was really good. The staff was naturally in their element—you can tell they truly enjoy work. A lot of down to earth, fun, kind; yet professional people. The pizza we ordered was great. The guy at the bar was personable and proactive. We bowled for 2 hours and had to stop maybe about 8 times; the balls kept getting stuck and waiting for the balls wasn’t even an issue because the music was on point. We danced as we waited. Truly enjoyed the music and the atmosphere. Each time there was a technical issue, more time was added to our time frame for free. And we appreciated that. My review will always be transparent; yet fair. 5 stars for me; highly recommend. Good service and good people are what makes businesses run smoothly. This place is worth every star. Hiccups happen, but how we go about resolving them speaks volumes.

I have come with large family a couple of times and we've had a great time each time! The restrooms are clean and the booths/lanes have also been clean. Good service as well. For a large place, they keep up with cleanliness and customer service. Overall, it's definitely a family-friendly environment.
It was nice, a small family oriented establishment. The menu appeared to be traditional, no complaints. And plenty of parking. Overall, 8/10. Definitely will visit again.

Had an absolute blast here! The atmosphere is electric, great music, and the lanes are well-maintained. We didn't have to wait long for a lane, and the staff was super attentive. Even the rental shoes were in good shape! Definitely our new go-to spot for a weekend hangout.

⭐️⭐️⭐️⭐️⭐️
Such a fun bowling experience!

I had an amazing time bowling here. From the moment we walked in, the vibe was upbeat and welcoming. The lanes were clean and well-maintained, the music kept the energy up, and everything ran smoothly without long waits or issues.

The staff was friendly, helpful, and made sure we had everything we needed, which really added to the experience. Whether you’re there to actually compete or just have fun and laugh with friends, it’s the perfect spot. Great atmosphere, great service, and nonstop fun.

Definitely a place I’d come back to and recommend for a fun night out, celebrations, or just something different to do 🎳✨

I recently went to Main Event to celebrate my nephew’s birthday, and we honestly had such a great time. I wasn’t sure what to expect since it was a Saturday night, but we ended up coming at the perfect time—right before the big crowd started rolling in. That made a huge difference because we were able to check in, get settled, and start bowling without any waiting or chaos. If you’re planning a visit, especially for a weekend night, I definitely recommend getting there a little early like we did.

The bowling part was a blast. The lanes were clean, the lighting was fun but not too distracting, and the music kept the energy high without being overbearing. It had that perfect mix of excitement and family-friendly vibes. The kids had a great time using the bumpers and lighter bowling balls, and even the adults got into a little friendly competition. The equipment worked perfectly, and when we needed help setting up the game, the staff was right there and super friendly. You could tell they were used to handling birthday parties and big groups—they made everything feel smooth and easy.

After a few rounds of bowling, we headed over to the arcade area, which was just as much fun, maybe even more. The variety of games was impressive—everything from racing simulators and air hockey to basketball hoops and the always-popular claw machines. The kids could’ve spent hours in there (and honestly, I think they did!). One of my personal favorites was the photobooth. It’s such a simple thing, but it ended up being a highlight of the night. We squeezed in as many people as we could and took a bunch of silly pictures that had everyone laughing. It was such a fun way to capture memories from the night, and the photo strips made for a great little keepsake.

The prize store was another big hit. The kids loved trading in their tickets and carefully deciding which prizes to get. The store had a great selection—everything from candy and fidget toys to bigger prizes for people who racked up more tickets. Watching my nephew’s face light up as he picked out his prize was priceless. It really made the whole experience feel complete.

We didn’t get a chance to eat a full meal there, but we did grab a few snacks and drinks between games. The service was friendly, though by the time the evening rush hit, the lines got noticeably longer. That’s honestly the only reason I’m giving this place four stars instead of five. Once it got later into the night, it became pretty crowded and noisy, and it took longer to get food or play certain games. It wasn’t unmanageable, but you could definitely feel the difference between when we arrived and when we left.

That said, even with the crowd, the energy was still really positive. The staff stayed upbeat and patient, which I really appreciated. You can tell Main Event puts a lot of effort into making it a fun, welcoming place for families and groups of all ages.

All in all, it was an awesome experience. Between the bowling, the arcade games, the photobooth, and the prize store, there was never a dull moment. My nephew and his friends had an amazing birthday, and everyone left smiling (and maybe a little tired from all the excitement). I can definitely see us coming back again—maybe for another birthday, or just a fun weekend outing. Next time, though, we’ll make sure to come early again to beat the evening rush.

If you’re looking for a place that combines games, food, and great vibes for all ages, Main Event is definitely worth checking out

I’ve come to this location several times and always have more fun than I expect. The food is good, but we try to not spend too much on that and buy more gameplays instead. It seems like a fun place to have a birthday party. The bowling lanes are always clean and I haven’t had any issues with the machines. You can bowl, play laser tag, and mini golf with someone or alone. The arcade games are also fun and there’s a generous amount of multiplayer options.

Celebrated a birthday here. Party of 14+. Our waitress, Morgan, was kind and attentive. Ordered Loaded Queso for appetizer, Smashed Burger Taco for entree and Tropical Rum Punch for cocktails. Had two lanes for bowling. Will return for other events.

Huge thanks to Chante, Kade, and Alex for helping with my daughter’s party! They made everything so easy and stress-free, from start to finish. Their attention to detail and friendly service made all the difference.

Definitely a place for family to have fun. Enjoy eating along with video games, pool, and  bowling. The waiters cater to your needs while you enjoy your game of bowling. Tacos weren't that great but the hamburger was good.

name: Times Square Entertainment
address: 402 W Grand Pkwy S #110, Katy, TX 77494, USA

reviews.text:
Excellent option for bowling, probably one of the best in town. There are also arcades and laser tag.
Make sure you buy your tickets in advance for a better and faster experience.
Kaylee was very helpful. She made our experience very nice. This place is very affordable and fun. We will be returning.
I took my daughter and grandkids. It was a great way to spend the day. The lanes were good and the kids had a great time.

I came here on a Friday evening with my kids. It's a decent play but I think price wise it was expensive. We arrived at 6.06pm and seems price of everything blows up past 6pm. Spent $80 to buy 2 cards for Arcade and it was gone within the hour. Didn't think we got value for money spent. Maybe timing was wrong. I do not recommend this place if you are on a budget and want more value for your bucks.

It’s is a reasonable price and nice place to take your family. On Monday I decided to go celebrate my daughter’s birthday there and a worker rubbed me wrong. When we arrived we noticed a lot of people were there didn’t think anything of it. When we got to the door we were immediately asked if we were there for the invite mind you nobody else was asked before going in and I realized why. But anyway, I told him no and he informed me they had an event until 8p. We could come back later or another day but we weren’t going in now. So I asked why wasn’t it listed on the website, he then said he have complimentary cards for inconvenience because the people booked in less than 48 hours but he couldn’t give every one in the group a card. My cousin was there with her 5 kids and I brought 5 kids with me he only gave 3 cards. So I asked why everyone couldn’t receive a card if we all were turned around. He proceeded to say our group was too large. So I then said if there wasn’t a event we would’ve been able to come in and spend our money for our large group which was 12 ppl (2 adults, 10 kids with one being 1 years old) he said yes but these cards aren’t for big groups. If yall inconvenienced me from coming spend my money why can’t you compensate the whole group to return back. Then he only gave the other adult 3 not say let me give you 3 and her 3. His tone and attitude was very rude. I hope yall find someone else to direct customers because he have a bad disposition which make don’t want to return

Fantastic fun bowling alley zero gravity games tickets billiards lots of fun. Had a lot of good times clean area clean bathrooms get a chance to try the food but if it’s like the other main event location, it’s above average.

Always come here to bowl for cheap, it’s the closest alley to me and it helps stay active with bowling. Summer passes are literally free bowling if u utilize it correctly.

You can make your summer passes pay off in 2 Visits and it lasts roughly 3 months. I have been getting it for the past 4 years and it’s amazing

Food is good and service can get slow sometimes but overall it’s a good experience
ShiAsia was a great server ensuring all of our needs were met and our party was taken care of. Todd did a great job checking on us throughout a time here. Me and my family will be back.

Julie was an amazing waitress! She was very attentive, kind, and sweet. She checked on us multiple times ensuring that all of our needs were met. The food was fresh, hot, and came out quick! Todd the manager was very personable and even stopped by to chat with us. He has a great personality. Thanks for making our experience here a great one!!!

I had an amazing experience at Main Event! The bowling was so much fun, with perfectly maintained lanes and a great atmosphere. The arcade was a blast, offering a variety of games that kept everyone entertained for hours. Our party hostess Frankie was exceptional, she was making sure everything ran smoothly and that we had everything we needed. The customer service was top-notch; the staff was friendly, attentive, and made us feel welcome from start to finish. And the food? Absolutely delicious! From appetizers to entrees, everything was cooked to perfection and really hit the spot. Overall, it was a fantastic experience, and I can’t wait to go back! Highly recommend it for anyone looking for fun, great service, and tasty food.

Frankie thank you so much for your helping us with my daughter’s birthday party. My daughter was very happy and had lots of fun.
Loved and enjoyed it here. Always clean and employees are always very nice. Will definitely keep coming back.
This place was incredible, very nice employees and the food was great. I will be back
Lanes were great, oiled and all you can play for 15.00 after 10pm!  Staff friendly and welcoming. We enjoyed the family time!

I've been bowling here at Max Bowl for about 5 months and during that time I have found a place to call my second home. The staff and faculty are so attentive to your needs and strive for excellence in customer service. Mrs. Janie is the best at serving her patrons and making sure the experience of family oriented leisure activities are operating at peak performance. Whether you're bringing the kids to bowl, playing at the arcade, or enjoy some top notch food at a reasonable price. We have enjoyed some memorable times here at Max Bowl Baytown and are thrilled of more great memories to come. I never thought I would be a big fan of bowling but this place has made it so easy to keep on coming back on our free time. My only regret about this place is that in all my 42 years of living in Baytown I did not find this place sooner. I want to give a shout out to Mr. Murray, he runs the Pro Shop, and is very knowledgeable on how to improve your game. He has a love for the sport of bowling and has a teaching spirit. Also the staff which includes Robert, Alex, Mike, Zack, Chevy, Mickey, and Jasmine. If I missed anyone it's because I probably haven't had a chance to meet you yet.
The culture of this place is so positive because of these great associates.
Mrs. Janie, thank you for welcoming my family and I to our new home.
Literally the best bowl center in the North East Houston area ! Love coming here with family and they offer so many things other than bowling.

name: Bowlero Webster
address: 318 W Bay Area Blvd, Webster, TX 77598, USA

reviews.text: We’ve been loyal customers since this location was AMF and have continued coming ever since it became Bowlero — over 30 years of great memories! We had the pleasure of meeting the manager, Isaiah, who was a phenomenal host and made sure everything ran smoothly. Our waiter, Johnathen, was patient, attentive, and very kind with our 55+ group. We highly recommend this location and will definitely keep coming back for birthdays, family gatherings, and fun nights out!

We just had my fiancés birthday party here for friends and family. Destiny the manager and our server John were incredible. I can’t tell you enough how attentive and wonderful they were. The food was delicious, and they decorated and made it adorable. It was such a great time and they accommodated all of our needs. We will be back!

Well, what really can I say about this place other than it is absolutely wonderful? How each lane is beautifully sectioned one right after the other, and every bowling ball is perfectly leveled and polished to such precise detail. How great and exquisite the food, drink, and accompanying accoutrements truly are.
But all that is pale in comparison to the amazing service provided by all the waiting staff, but most importantly, all the incredible management, such as the one and only Brooke Showman.

My son had been doing the homeschool bowling group these last few weeks, so we figured we would all go as a family today (it had been over 20 years since my husband and I have been bowling). It was so much fun! The environment is great, everyone is friendly. There is even a ramp that you can use if you have littles, my 3-year-old was offered that by the owner i believe (very nice as well)! Our server was Paul, he went above and beyond and has awesome customer service...exactly something I always appreciate and was very nice to my kids too. Can't wait to go back for another family day! The pizza was fresh and very good, refills are FREE!

I saw the bad reviews and decided to try it anyway because my husband and I NEVER get a date night and we really wanted to bowl. Let me start by telling you the pricing is absolutely insane on a Saturday night. The sign when you walk in is very misleading as well, it states "11.99 per game 5.99 shoe rental" then it states buy 2 games get one 50% off" however it does not state that this is per person, so for my husband and I to play 3 games and get 2 shoe rentals, it was $77, which is sickening. Then we order a pitcher of beer which was $26. We ordered cheese fries to share at 7:02 and we never received them. We kept asking the kitchen where they were and apparently they gave them to someone else after telling us it was just a busy night. At 8:15pm we find a manager and tell him the situation. We are done bowling at this point. We even spent another $25 for one more game to kill time waiting on the fries. The manager says he can refund the fries, but not the tip. So we ended up spending well over $150 at a place that couldn't even get us our food. I felt we deserved more compensation, but they didn't care. Do not recommend, just go to Main Event.

name: Bowlero Stafford
address: 4919 S Main St, Stafford, TX 77477, USA

reviews.text: We had an excellent experience at the sponsored event. The staff was friendly, attentive, and quick to help both at the front desk and the bar. The food was outstanding, served hot and full of flavor, and far above what we’ve found at comparable venues. It was absolutely worth the drive.

Very nice place to go. I enjoyed going here for the first time. They allow you to reserve a lane and they bring any food and drinks to you. Everything you need is right in your lane. I would definitely recommend this place. It's a must try!

It was a great place to bowl and they had pretty good snacks and service. The lanes are kind of tight so it felt like we were on top of the folks on either lane of us, but other than that it was a good experience. Oh actually the fire alarm kept going off while we were there which was kind of annoying, but we still bowled 3 games and had fun.

Last night I went to Bowlero and ordered a drink, and the experience was extremely uncomfortable. The server was rude from the moment he approached us. When he asked for my ID, I gave him my passport. He clearly watched me pull it out of my purse, which already had the seal on it. A passport is a book, and I opened it directly in front of him to the page with my information. Instead of simply checking it, he disrespectfully closed the book, looked under it, twisted his body, held it up to the light, and said, “I just wanted to make sure it was real.” He then looked me up and down, rolled his eyes, and pretended to examine it further.

What made this worse is that he did not check the ID of the other person I was with, who is of a different nationality than me. I did not question him at first, but the interaction felt very microaggressive and discriminatory. I asked the server to get a manager. He went to the office and returned saying that was the only manager available. The manager showed no concern for my complaint and walked away. As he did, the server looked at him, rolled his eyes, and the manager gave him a thumbs up, clearly signaling that it was not a big deal.

Later, I found out there were two other managers on the floor that night, both Black women, and he specifically did not get either of them. It felt very intentional, as if he knew he was wrong. Neither the server nor the manager apologized, and my complaint was completely dismissed. This was a disgusting experience, and I will be contacting corporate. Being treated this way and having discrimination waved off as acceptable is not okay.
Great place for bowling. Their setup is good, ambience is great, you can also order food if you like. Spacious - can accomodate large groups as well. Clean as well.

Great place to take the family for a night of fun. It's clean, well organized and has up-to-date equipment.
The reason for 4 stars and not 5 was really only their bowling ball selection (I have big fingers).
I will definitely be back, we paid less for our family of 4 to bowl a game than anywhere we've been and we had a great time!
We love it here. Had a good time. Me my son and my girlfriend and her kid.

This man has the WORST customer service I've seen in a long time. He told me to wait ✋️ while he watched other people bowl before he would help me. Then told me he would not correct my score after one of the employees walked down the labe beside me while I was bowling. I really don't know why my family even comes here. They act extremely rude to African Americans!

I like this place, it might be busy at times, but we still go. There is time I can't find my size bowling ball, but I know the keep at least one. Lol. The staff is friendly and very helpful. They have a small cafe here, and it pretty good. We enjoy it every time. They keep this place clean, and shoes are kept clean. Restrooms are available and clean, too. We're not big-time bowlers, but we enjoyed it one in a while.

Took my wifes family here to have a good time but was instantly met with disappointment when we walked in to rude customed service. No one has name tags so I only know him as the "man with grey hair" but he just seemed like he didnt even want to be there. I can always tell when someone just wants you out of their presence and it felt like we werent welcomed there.
We decided to order some food. The kids got 2 pizzas for $10 each and 3 pitchers of sodas for $5 each. Pizza was overcooked, tasted expired, and looked like they had just boughten the cheapest box pizza they could get from the nearest gas station. Even little ceasers has better pizza than this for only $6. The pitchers of soda were also 70% filled with ice, definitely not worth it.
Overall, we were not satisfied with our experience. It was like one negative experience after another at this location. Luckily we only played 1 game at left right away.
Save your money and just go somewhere like main event.

Enjoyed a family outing here. Good experience and prices. Clean bowling although a bit older. They were playing a variety of tunes
. Made enjoyable. A bit understaffed but thinking about the holiday. Will come back. Has a senior rate

Since they don’t have any details anywhere on their website or online about rates or specials, you have to call the establishment, which all in itself is fine, until they give you the completely wrong information, and you make plans to come on a Saturday and the man at the front keeps reminding you at what time they close. If y’all are going to close at a different time than what’s stated online, then update your information. Experience felt rushed and like they didn’t want us there. This is why establishments like this are being replaced by mass marketed companies because they’re not appreciating the people that do come out.
Could use a little TLC but we had a great time! Staff was over all pretty nice. Bartender was super sweet and working super hard. Drinks were cheap and strong 😬 we had a great time!

Armadilla lanes is an oldie but goodie for people who grew up around here. It has seen many generations. I especially like the people at the snack bar because they are really sweet. My grandchildren love it there!

The front desk people are so rude when you ask them a question,  i don”t understand why but overall besides the front desk everything is fun and entertaining accept the fact you can”t bring your own drinks in
